opps.title: NIH NICHD OIT Application and Website Support Services
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Custom Computer Programming (web/software — MBDA/Census lane)
opps.description:
The NICHD Office of Information Technology  requires continued planning and technical support for IT application and website development and maintenance, Section 508 Compliance, database and data mart support, application infrastructure support, and other technical services including but not limited to the support of new and emerging technologies such as Artificial Intelligence (AI), Machine learning (ML)/Robotic Process Automation (RPA), NICHD and/or NIH Wide. The focus of these IT support activities is creation, development, maintenance, operation, and support of a web application infrastructure that provides an efficient, effective, reliable, and secure computing environment in support of NICHD's and NIH's mission and supports staff in the efficient and effective utilization of these capabilities. 

Details on the evaluation criteria are contained in the attached RFQ.

In order to be considered for award, a vendor will be required to cover the SIN(s) listed below awarded on their Schedule contract. Any quote received from a contractor that does not cover the required SIN(s) will not be further evaluated and subsequently ineligible for award. Required SIN(s): 	54151S (Information Technology Professional Services); 541611 (Management and Financial Consulting, Acquisition and Grants Management Support, and Business Program and Project Management Services)
opps.summary:
Background
 The National Institutes of Health (NIH), specifically the Eunice Kennedy Shriver National Institute of Child Health and Human Development (NICHD), is seeking continued planning and technical support for IT application and website development and maintenance. The goal of this contract is to ensure Section 508 Compliance, provide database and data mart support, and offer application infrastructure support, including the integration of new technologies such as Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML).

opps.title: Section 508 Compliance Support Services
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Custom Computer Programming (web/software — MBDA/Census lane)
opps.description:
****This requirement will be a single award IDV so APFS is not required.  This is marked as no longer planned and republished for vendor awareness.****

CISA National Risk Management Center (NRMC) seeks Section 508 compliance support services to assist the organization in ensuring information and communications technology (ICT), electronic content, digital communications, and associated documentation comply with Section 508 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973, as amended (29 U.S.C. Â§ 794d), and applicable accessibility standards.

Services will include accessibility evaluations, remediation, testing support, documentation review, compliance reporting, consultation, and guidance related to accessibility best practices and federal accessibility requirements. The requirement will support the organization in identifying accessibility deficiencies, reducing compliance risk, and improving accessibility for individuals with disabilities through remediation actions.

Technical support for electronic documents, websites, web applications, software platforms, multimedia products, training materials, and other digital content produced or maintained by the organization will be required.   Section 508 conformance testing of web content and web applications using the DHS Trusted Tester Section 508 Conformance Test Process for Web and evaluation and remediation of electronic documents (e.g., Microsoft Office and PDF) to meet Section 508 requirements using federally recognized accessibility testing methods for documents will be required.

opps.title: CISA Stakeholder Engagement and Analytics Support Services
opps.description: The Stakeholder Engagement Division (SED) requires strategic relations, stakeholder engagement, analytics, and program support services to advance CISAâ€™s mission through coordinated outreach, partnership development, awareness initiatives, stakeholder data management, and performance evaluation. SED serves as CISAâ€™s focal point for enterprise stakeholder engagement, supporting relationships with federal, state, local, tribal, and territorial governments, critical infrastructure partners, private industry, academia, non-profit organizations, and the public. Support includes planning and executing stakeholder engagement strategies; managing awareness and outreach campaigns; supporting Systemically Important Entities (SIE) activities; facilitating Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure Act (CIRCIA) implementation efforts; supporting cybersecurity workforce and academic partnership initiatives; and maintaining stakeholder collaboration and information-sharing activities. The contractor shall provide expertise in stakeholder analysis, program evaluation, data governance, performance measurements, communications, and project management, including support for CISAâ€™s Stakeholder Relationship Management (SRM) platform, analytics, reporting and visualization capabilities. Support shall include collection, integration, analysis, and reporting of stakeholder and program data to assess outcomes, improve decision-making, identity trend, and enhance mission effectiveness. Work will be performed at a combination of contractor facilities and the Department of Homeland Security facilities as required.

opps.title: INTEGRATED PROGRAM MANAGEMENT AND INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY SERVICES
opps.description:
The Coast Guard Accessions Service Center (CG ASC) seeks contractor support to enhance recruiting operations through program management, analytical services, and advanced information technology. A key focus of this requirement is the development, deployment, and sustainment of custom business applications using Microsoft Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI). The contractor will deliver business process automation solutions, including Robotic Process Automation (RPA), to streamline operations, improve efficiency, and support data-driven decision-making.

Additionally, the contractor will provide graphic design and content creation services to support both internal and external communications. This includes designing user interfaces for applications, developing marketing materials, and producing visually engaging content for outreach and stakeholder engagement. All content must align with Coast Guard branding and approval protocols.

Other services include planning and analysis led by Project Management Professional (PMP) certified personnel, performance management, strategic planning, meeting facilitation, documentation, process improvement, change coordination, reporting, and knowledge management. Personnel must have relevant certifications and government experience.

The period of performance is one base year plus four option years. Work will primarily occur at Coast Guard Headquarters in Washington DC, with possible off-site work as approved. All contractor staff must meet pre-employment screening and background investigation requirements.

opps.title: Power Platform Development Support for Office of Emergency Management
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Custom Computer Programming (web/software — MBDA/Census lane)
opps.description:
The Department of the Interior (DOI), Interior Business Center (IBC), Acquisition Services Directorate (AQD), is conducting market research on behalf of the Office of Emergency Management (OEM). 

OEM relies on Microsoft Power Platform, SharePoint, PowerApps, Power Automate, and Dataverse for mission-critical emergency management operations including incident reporting, resource tracking, workflow automation, and continuity of operations support. AQD is seeking information from qualified vendors capable of supporting the development, modernization, and sustainment of these solutions in a federal environment.

Vendors with demonstrated experience in Power Platform development, Application Lifecycle Management (ALM), Microsoft Graph API integration, Section 508 compliance, and federal IT security standards are encouraged to respond.

Please review ALL attached documents before responding, including the Request for Information (RFI) and the DOI Information Technology Baseline Compliance Contract Guidelines. 

All submission requirements, capability areas of interest, and response instructions are detailed in the attached RFI.

Responses are due by July 9, 2026 11:00 AM Eastern.

All responses must be submitted in PDF format via email to Candy_Mott-Harris@ibc.doi.gov with the subject line: RFI Response OEM Power Platform Development Support.

opps.title: Communication Support
opps.description:
1 Scope
The purpose of this Statement of Work (SOW) is to procure professional video production, content creation, and communication support services for the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), Office of Public Affairs (OPA). The services provided by the Contractor must enhance the FBI’s social media communication strategies, increase public awareness of FBI operational successes, and provide the public with actionable information to help prevent crime and fraud. The Contractor must provide all personnel, equipment, tools, materials, supervision, and other items necessary to perform video production and communication services as described in this SOW.
This is a non-personal services contract. The Government shall not exercise any supervision or control over contractor employees performing the services herein. Such contract service providers shall be accountable solely to the Contractor who, in turn is responsible to the Government.
1.1 Description of Services/Introduction
The FBI’s OPA requires video production and communication services to support FBI efforts in ensuring the effectiveness of existing social media communication strategies and content creation. Services will include the use of modern communication methods to increase the public’s awareness of key FBI operational successes and provide actionable information the public can use to protect themselves from becoming a victim of crime and fraud.
The Contractor must provide video and content creation services to support FBI social media communications efforts across the following key areas:
• Repurpose publicly available content to create compelling video imagery designed to be shared and promoted over social media platforms.
The Contractor must ensure the personnel providing the labor hours possess the knowledge, skills, and ability necessary to address the applicable Revised 508 Standards defined in this contract and shall provide supporting documentation upon request. When provid

opps.description:
***03/05/2026: Estimated solicitation release date updated to 03/31/2026, estimated period of performance start date updated to 09/16/2026, and anticipated award date updated to 09/01/2026.***
***01/13/2025: Estimated solicitation release date updated to 03/02/2026.***

***9/24/2025: Estimated solicitation release date updated from 09/11/2025 to 11/03/2025; Dollar Range updated from $10M-$20M to $5M-$10M.***

USCIS has a requirement for Continuous Monitoring & Agile Security Services (CMASS).  USCIS seeks to establish a more sustainable and effective continuous monitoring program, leveraging data from security and governance tools to enhance our ability to identify and mitigate cybersecurity risks, evaluate emerging technologies, streamline security-related tasks. Additionally, we seek to transform the Ongoing Authorization (OA) program to incorporate new capabilities and risk models for Machine Learning (ML) and Artificial Intelligence (AI) and implement appropriate governance measures. The CMASS resources will continue to support security authorization and continuous monitoring activities, the Vulnerability Management & Configuration Management Programs, role-based cybersecurity training, and system stakeholder outreach to ensure USCIS meets security and DHS Information System Performance Plan (ISPP) requirements.

opps.title: Immigration Biometrics and Identity Services (IBIS)
opps.description: The combined USCIS Immigration Biometrics and Identity Services (IBIS) project will deliver secure, scalable, and modern IT solutions supporting data analytics, machine learning, entity resolution, biometrics and identity management, and scheduling for the nationâ€™s immigration system. Leveraging agile methodologies and advanced Development, Security, and Operations (DevSecOps) practices, multi-disciplinary teams develop, operate, and maintain cloud-based applications using a robust suite of open-source and commercial tools. The project emphasizes automation, continuous integration and delivery, and strict adherence to federal security, privacy, and accessibility standards. Teams are responsible for end-to-end development, operations, security, and technical support, ensuring high-quality code, rapid incident response, and comprehensive documentation. This integrated approach enables USCIS to efficiently and securely manage complex identity and biometrics systems for both internal and public-facing applications.

opps.title: Video Production Professional Staffing
opps.description:
The Department of State's Bureau of Global Public Affairs (GPA), Office of Video (GPA/DC/VID), is soliciting proposals for Digital Video Staffing Services (DVDS) in support of its mission to communicate U.S. foreign policy priorities to domestic and global audiences. The contractor shall provide a professional staff of approximately 2550 personnel to conceive, produce, and deliver high-quality video, broadcast, and live interactive web content across digital and traditional media platforms worldwide. Services span the full production lifecycle from pre-production planning and scripting to post-production editing and global live broadcast origination and must be executed in an agile, responsive manner consistent with the fast-paced demands of a news-style environment.

The scope of work encompasses three primary task areas: (1) Professional Digital Media Production, including social media video, motion graphics, and documentary-style content; (2) Digital Broadcast, Interactive, and Live Streaming, including studio operations, live coverage of Department press briefings, and Secretary-level engagements; and (3) Video Broadcast Infrastructure Maintenance and Technology Services, including procurement, maintenance, and support of broadcast and IT equipment and software subscriptions. Contractor personnel must be willing to travel domestically and internationally on short notice, work nights and weekends as required, and maintain constant readiness to support time-sensitive tasking. Performance will be conducted primarily at the Department's headquarters at 2201 C Street, NW, Washington, D.C.

Please see all attachments included with this RFP for full details, technical requirements, performance standards, and contractual specifications governing this acquisition. Offerors are responsible for reviewing all attached documents prior to submitting a proposal.

All questions regarding this solicitation must be submitted in writing to the Contracting Officers at HofhineAK@sta

May 12, 2026 The U.S. General Services Administration’s Office of Acquisition Solutions Development (ASD) is pleased to announce the first Phase II apparent awardee notifications for OASIS+ 8(a) Small Business Set-Aside. A consolidated list of Phase II apparent awardees for all OASIS+ contract families may be accessed on the Solicitations (continuously open) page (see “Links” section within this SAM.gov posting). This list will be updated with additional apparent awardees as award announcements continue to be made.

Award notifications have been sent via email to offerors who have been determined successful in one or more proposed domains. Apparent awardees are reminded to ensure that their SAM.gov entity information is active, accurate, and complete at all times. The program plans to issue formal awards and notices to proceed (NTPs) to qualified contractors on or about May 22, 2026 after final legal reviews occur. The newly awarded contracts will be available for ordering in GSA eBuy one business day following the issuance of formal awards and NTPs and upon contractor registration within the Vendor Support Center (VSC), whichever is later. Contract awardees must register their contracts in VSC before they will be visible in eBuy and available for procurement. 

Please note that GSA continues to release answers to RFP questions deemed broadly beneficial and relevant on a regular basis on the program website Solicitations (continuously open) page. Questions related to the award announcements should be submitted via email to the OASIS+ Contracting Officers at oasisplusmods@gsa.gov.

Please continue to monitor this OASIS+ Interact Community forum where you can join and subscribe to get notifications about announcements and program updates. For more information about the OASIS+ Program, visit: The OASIS+ website. Also, follow our social media channels X and LinkedIn. 

************************************************************************************

opps.title: NOS Cloud Migration & Data Infrastructure Support
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Other Computer Services (Census DWS lane)
opps.description: Comprehensive data management support to develop and implement a modern, interoperable ocean data infrastructure that will facilitate cloud migration, simplify data management, and accelerate AI integration across the organization. The core objective of this contract is to transition from traditional data architectures to a unifying multi-cloud environment, establishing governance standards and foundational support for AI development, including designing for the MLOps Lifecycle and simplifying AI access. This initiative is crucial for advancing NOS’s cloud-first strategy, supporting projects like marine critical minerals, and ensuring functional management of data and metadata. Potential vendors should structure their proposals to provide expertise in infrastructure engineering, data governance, and cloud architecture, covering positions such as Cloud Architect, Data Center Administrator, Data Technician, and IT Engineer.

BSEE Data Management Services - Gulf of America and Pacific Regions Oil and Gas Experience. 

The Department of the Interior (DOI), Bureau of Safety and Environmental Enforcement (BSEE) is issuing this competitive Request for Quote (RFQ) to eligible General Services Administration (GSA) Federal Supply Schedule (FSS) Contract holders under GSA Schedule  54151S, "Information Technology Professional Services IT Professional Services and/or labor categories for database planning and design; systems analysis, integration, and design; programming, conversion and implementation support; network services, data/records management, and testing" for the purpose of entering into a Task Order for BSEE Data Management Services.  

The objective of the task order is to provide data and information management support services to the BSEE Gulf of America Region's various program offices (primary site), the BSEE Pacific Regions' program offices (secondary site).  The Contractor will provide high, mid, and entry level data and information management expertise to support data collection, analysis, imaging, and retrieval/delivery services.  Other services will include project management, technical consulting, and outreach services.  The requirements will encompass such functions as data entry, data loading, document processing, document indexing, document scanning, data validation, data quality assurance, data archiving, and overseeing projects.  

See attached RFQ 140E0126Q0028.

opps.title: Program Evaluation and Performance Management Services for the Sector Risk Management Agency (SRMA)
opps.description:
***This requirement is not planned for FY26.  However, there may be a follow-on in early FY27; record will be updated to reflect those changes.  Republishing for vendor awareness.***

The Stakeholder Engagement Division (SED) is requiring Program Evaluation and Performance Management Services for the Sector Risk Management Agency (SRMA).   The SRMA Program requires identification and guidelines for assessing the criticality and efficiency/efficacy of the SRMAs and responding to reduce risk to critical infrastructure by coordination through State, local, tribal, and territorial (SLTT) governments, Federal Agencies and industry partners. The approaches for completion are various but jointly developed within and among the critical infrastructure sectors and SRMAs. The execution of the Sector Management role depends on the specific sector. It is influenced by external factors such as sector composition and profile, sector landscape, risk management maturity, and regulatory framework. The Sector Management role is also influenced by internal factors such as the type and extent of programs executed by other CISA divisions and CISA Regional Offices that may be more focused on some sectors than others or may have direct or quantifiable benefits in support of the sector. Work will be performed at a combination of the Contractorâ€™s facilities and the Department of Homeland Security at 4200 Wilson Boulevard, Arlington, Virginia.

opps.title: USCG Financial Service Center (FSC) Automation and Communication Support
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Custom Computer Programming (web/software — MBDA/Census lane)
opps.description: The contractor will be responsible for a comprehensive range of services to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of the USCG Financial Service Center's operations. This involves the full lifecycle of automation, from analyzing current financial accounting processes to designing and developing new automated solutions using tools such as the Microsoft 365 Power Platform, UiPath, and SharePoint. Alongside development, the contractor must provide continuous sustainment and operational support for all automated systems, which includes monitoring, administration, troubleshooting, and user assistance to ensure their reliability. Furthermore, the role requires supporting government-led initiatives to standardize and improve business processes by mapping workflows, identifying control gaps, and creating a library of documentation like Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), in addition to developing and delivering training programs. Finally, the contractor will handle general support tasks, including the creation of detailed project documentation, reporting on Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) to measure the impact of automation, and managing communications with all stakeholders regarding system updates and enhancements.

opps.title: One Acquisition Solution for Integrated Services Plus (OASIS+) Total Small Business Set-Aside Continuously Open
opps.description:
May 12, 2026 The U.S. General Services Administration’s Office of Acquisition Solutions Development (ASD) is pleased to announce the first Phase II apparent awardee notifications for OASIS+ Total Small Business Set-Aside. A consolidated list of Phase II apparent awardees for all OASIS+ contract families may be accessed on the Solicitations (continuously open) page (see “Links” section within this SAM.gov posting). This list will be updated with additional apparent awardees as award announcements continue to be made.

Award notifications have been sent via email to offerors who have been determined successful in one or more proposed domains. Apparent awardees are reminded to ensure that their SAM.gov entity information is active, accurate, and complete at all times. The program plans to issue formal awards and notices to proceed (NTPs) to qualified contractors on or about May 22, 2026 after final legal reviews occur. The newly awarded contracts will be available for ordering in GSA eBuy one business day following the issuance of formal awards and NTPs and upon contractor registration within the Vendor Support Center (VSC), whichever is later. Contract awardees must register their contracts in VSC before they will be visible in eBuy and available for procurement. 

Please note that GSA continues to release answers to RFP questions deemed broadly beneficial and relevant on a regular basis on the program website Solicitations (continuously open) page. Questions related to the award announcements should be submitted via email to the OASIS+ Contracting Officers at oasisplusmods@gsa.gov.

Please continue to monitor this OASIS+ Interact Community forum where you can join and subscribe to get notifications about announcements and program updates. For more information about the OASIS+ Program, visit: The OASIS+ website. Also, follow our social media channels X and LinkedIn. 

***********************************************************************************

opps.title: Accelerated Constituent Service Solutions
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Custom Computer Programming (web/software — MBDA/Census lane)
opps.description:
The Government is seeking industry feedback on a DRAFT Master Blanket Purchase Agreement (BPA) for Accelerated Constituent Service Solutions. The purpose of this Request for Information (RFI) is to obtain comments, recommendations, and suggested revisions from industry to help refine the DRAFT BPA structure, scope, requirements, ordering procedures, and related terms prior to any potential solicitation.

The contemplated BPA is intended to provide The General Services Administration (GSA) with a streamlined ordering mechanism for the licensing, configuration, technical delivery, integration, and support of secure digital platform solutions. These solutions would enable agencies to rapidly develop and deploy production-ready, constituent-facing applications within their existing authorized cloud environments and security boundaries. Potential use cases may include, but are not limited to, digital forms, identity and access capabilities, case management, payment functionality, workflow automation, reporting, and other mission-specific digital services.

Through this RFI, the Government is particularly interested in industry perspectives on how best to structure the BPA to support rapid delivery, scalability, security, interoperability, and flexibility across diverse agency missions. Respondents are encouraged to identify areas of the DRAFT BPA that may benefit from clarification, recommend commercial best practices, highlight potential barriers to competition or performance, and propose approaches that would improve the Government's ability to acquire and deploy secure, user-centered digital services efficiently.

The Government's objective is to establish a BPA that supports the delivery of fully operational, production-ready constituent-facing applications, allowing agencies to stand up mission-specific services quickly while maintaining compliance with applicable security, privacy, accessibility, and cloud authorization requirements. Industry feedback received in r

opps.title: Common API Services (CAS)
opps.description:
The Internal Revenue Service (IRS), Information Technology (IT) Data & Platform Engineering (D&PE) organization provides enterprise platforms, Application Programming Interfaces (API), data services, and identity management capabilities that enable secure, scalable, and reusable solutions across IRS systems. These capabilities reduce duplication, lower risk and cost, and accelerate delivery of modernization initiatives.

The D&PE API Services organization requires contractor support to modernize, operate, and sustain mission-critical middleware applications and shared services that support tax processing, taxpayer services, and other IRS mission-essential functions.

The objective of the Performance Work Statement (PWS) is to:

-	Modernize legacy middleware platforms, including webMethods Enterprise Service Bus (ESB), Bulk Request Management Gateway (BRMG), and Business Rules Engine (BRE), into microservices-based architectures using Spring Boot and OpenShift standards 
-	Maintain uninterrupted operations during transition 
-	Modernize common business services (APIs) and sustain existing services throughout the modernization efforts
-	Upgrade applications to current Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) versions and/or OpenShift (Enterprise Container Platform (ECP)

The IRS requires full lifecycle support (analysis, development, testing, and deployment) for applications hosted in IRS on-premise environments. This support includes delivery of software releases aligned to IRS Forms processing, modernization initiatives, and legislative requirements.
opps.summary:

The D&PE API Services organization seeks contractor support to modernize, operate, and sustain critical middleware applications and shared services

Product development areas include: statistical modeling, digital database development; model interpretation, verification and evaluation; cloud infrastructure development and support; storm surge prediction; Virtual Lab infrastructure development and support, and decision support application development (NWS Connect). opps.title: USACE UMSB Army Vantage Integration Field Service Representative (FRS)
opps.description: The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, USACE Mission Support Battalion (UMSB) is executing a directed modernization initiative to transition operational workflows from manual, locally managed processes to structured, enterprise-integrated systems aligned with Army Vantage and the Microsoft ecosystem. This effort supports federal and Army digital transformation mandates requiring measurable progress in data consolidation, automated reporting, and enterprise visibility. One (1) senior-level Field Service Representative (FSR) will need to be embedded at Humphreys Engineer Center, Alexandria, Virginia, with periodic presence at a secondary Government location in Washington, DC beginning in Month 4. The FSR shall perform duties consistent with the approved Performance Work Statement (PWS) across five task areas: backend integration support, workflow documentation and transition support, user training and adoption support, integration status reporting, and stakeholder engagement and coordination. The FSR shall provide on-site technical and change management support, working directly with workflow owners, UMSB leadership, and the UMSB Operations (OPS) office. The overarching program objective for the base period is to achieve the HR Integration End State as defined in Section 3 of the Performance Work Statement (PWS), establishing a replicable integration model for future directorate-level transitions. Leadership's primary operational objective is to utilize Army Vantage's built-in templated dashboard functions, populated from authoritative HR data sources, as the standard method for HR data visibility and decision support, replacing manual data pulls and locally managed reporting.

opps.title: Cyber Grant Support and Program Development
opps.description:
Summary of SOW Requirements: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS), via the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) Integrated Operations Division (IOD), requires the Contractor to deliver comprehensive support for the State and Local Cybersecurity Grant Program (SLCGP) and Tribal Cybersecurity Grant Program (TCGP). The Contractor will actively advance program development, maintain information continuity, and ensure operational excellence across CISA regions, fostering strong coordination with CISA Divisions, Mission Enabling Offices, and interagency partners.

Scope and Objectives: The Contractor is responsible for both administrative and programmatic support to IOD, facilitating communication, capacity building, and awareness of cybersecurity grant programs. The Contractor will coordinate efforts across CISA regions and headquarters, strengthen regional capabilities, support service delivery, and execute IOD priorities.

Key Tasks and Requirements:
1. Program Support and Development: Collect and analyze best practices, lessons learned, and requirements to drive continuous improvement and inform future grant opportunities. Provide relevant documents and facilitate delivery of grant programs across regions, enhancing cyber competency among security advisors, analysts, and outreach staff. Deliver training to regional staff, ensuring readiness and effective execution. Establish mechanisms to capture return on investment (ROI) through program monitoring, and manage technical assistance for Cybersecurity Advisors and State Coordinators. Support annual program monitoring and grant close-out activities.

2. Operational and Strategic Engagement: Maintain and enhance tools such as the Service Change Request Tool and IOD Intake Process to gather requirements, prioritize services, and transition to full operating capability. Improve dashboards and automate reporting, including integration with ServiceNow. Develop engagement plans and public-facing material

opps.title: Enterprise API Gateway (EAG).
opps.description:
The Internal Revenue Service (IRS), Information Technology (IT) Data & Platform Engineering Organization requires contractor support to modernize, automate, operate, and sustain the Enterprise API Gateway (EAG).

Under this effort, the Contractor shall provide full lifecycle technical services, including design, development, configuration, modernization, operations, and maintenance of the Enterprise API Gateway environment. 

An Application Programming Interface (API) is a defined set of rules and protocols that enables different software systems to communicate and exchange data, and an API gateway is a centralized service that manages, secures, routes, and monitors API traffic between providers and consumers. The Contractor shall ensure continuity of operations during the transition and maintain support for existing APIs while implementing modernization improvements.

The end products of this contract will include a modernized Enterprise API Gateway operating model which consists of:

-	Automated API onboarding and Continuous Integration /Continuous Deployment (CI/CD) pipelines
-	Enterprise API governance artifacts and standards
-	Security controls aligned with Zero Trust principles
-	Developer enablement documentation and tools
-	Integration with various Artificial Intelligence (AI) providers, consumers, and/or gateways
-	Sustained production operations and performance monitoring for the duration of the contract
The results of this contract will be used to improve system interoperability, standardize API lifecycle management, enhance cybersecurity, and support IRS modernization and mission delivery requirements.

opps.description:
This re-compete acquisition seeks comprehensive professional support services for the U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) Office of Information Technology (OIT)'s Management and Governance Directorate (MGD), Financial Management Acquisition Division (FMAD), and Strategic Planning & Mission Engagement Division (SPME). Services encompass strategy implementation, financial planning, and investment management, aiming to enhance OIT's budget formulation, planning, and reporting, and ensure transparent, data-driven financial decision-making. Key support areas include Capital Planning and Investment Control (CPIC) coordination, strategic financial planning, strategy management, and critical support for the Unified Immigration Portal (UIP) program, focusing on cost models and reporting. The requirement also involves extensive analytics, automation, data visualization, and platforms technical services, including the development and maintenance of executive dashboards (e.g., Power BI, Power Platform, Apptio Cloud ability). These services are essential for managing extensive financial resources and delivering mission-critical IT capabilities that support trade administration, traveler movement, and border security.

 Acquisition Strategy: 100% Small Business Set-Aside competed under General Services Administration (GSA) OASIS+ Small Business (SB) Pool.

opps.title: Program Management Office Support
opps.description: This requirement is for contractor support to provide three Agile-driven teams to deliver program management, risk management, and knowledge management services for the Student and Exchange Visitor Program (SEVP)systems and programs. The contractor will support performance measurement, process improvement, data analytics, requirements management, and business case development for SEVP operations. Responsibilities include developing and maintaining risk management plans for the Student and Exchange Visitor Information System (SEVIS) Programs Section and System Management Unit. The contractor support team will coordinate risk reviews, provide independent risk assessments, and manage risk inventories and mitigation strategies. Additionally, the contractor team will support the operational management of program documentation and knowledge libraries within the SEVP Assessment Management System (SEVPAMS), ensuring proper version control and metadata management. Strategic planning and administrative support will be provided to facilitate efficient operations, compliance with Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) privacy and information assurance policies, and continuous improvement of SEVP systems and processes. The scope excludes software development and focuses on management, administration, and support functions.

opps.title: Executive Training focusing on Communication
opps.description:
REQUIREMENT: Executive Leadership, Communication, & AI Integration Training
SET-ASIDE: 100% Total Small Business Set-Aside (FAR 8.405-5)
GSA SIN: 611430 (Professional and Management Development Training)

DESCRIPTION:
The Department of War (DoW) requires specialized, instructor-led training for executive-level personnel (supervisors and above). Standard, entry-level professional development courses will not be accepted.

The contractor shall provide customized training covering the following five (5) specific modules:

Module	Topic Focus
1	Communication Techniques
2      Communication Style
3	Adaptability and Change Management
4	Managing Difficult Conversations
5	Utilizing AI Tools with Confidence

KEY CONSTRAINT: To accommodate executive schedules, each individual training module shall NOT exceed 2.5 hours in length.

INSTRUCTIONS TO OFFERORS:
This requirement is being competed as a 100% Small Business Set-Aside. Only quotes from verified Small Business GSA Schedule holders under SIN 611430 will be evaluated.

Please review the attached Performance Work Statement (PWS) for full technical requirements and the Evaluation Criteria document for submission instructions. Quotes must be submitted as two distinct volumes (Technical and Price) exactly as outlined in the evaluation attachments.

Work Details

1) Communication Techniques

opps.description:
*** 5/19/2026: USCIS anticipates releasing a solicitation on NASA SEWP V set-aside to small business and anticipates solicitation release in early June. This requirement will be competed without any brand-name specifications.***

USCIS anticipate a requirement for a commercial software solution for document authentication and identity verification service. These services are essential forÂ maintainingÂ program integrity, supporting federal operations, and ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements. Historically, many USCIS processes have relied on manual, in-person document review, which can present operational challenges and limit access and flexibility,Â especially for remote or distributed users. More specifically, the solution will capture high quality images of identity and work authorization documents, apply Presentation Attack Detection (PAD) and face match checks, use template and barcode/MRZ based authentication, and extract structured data via Optical Character Recognition (OCR) for use in USCIS-controlled microservices. All processing will occur within USCIS or approved Department of Homeland Security (DHS) environments, in compliance with DHS security and privacy requirements.

opps.title: USCG CGLO PROGRAM ANALYST
opps.description:
U.S. Coast Guard (USCG) USCG Liaison Office, situated onboard Naval Air Station Pensacola, FL at 211 South Avenue is seeking a Program/Management Analyst to fulfill its operational needs. This is a Firm-Fixed Price order.

The requirements document in the form of a Statement of Work is attached.

Detailed information regarding the incumbent award can be accessed on https://www.usaspending.gov/, under Award ID: 70Z04325FSMTC0005.

This announcement constitutes the only solicitation; quotations are being requested.
Evaluation: A Firm-Fixed purchase order will be made to the contractor that represents the best value. Quotes will be evaluated based on technical approach, experience of key personnel, and price. Under the 'Experience of Key Personnel' factor, the government will consider the experience of the proposed key person and the estimated time required to train them on government-specific systems and procedures. Proposals that demonstrate the key person has relevant experience and requires minimal training will be viewed more favorably.

The anticipated award date is 10 days after solicitation closing date.

All quoters must submit the following: Submission of a quote that does not contain all items requested below may result in elimination from consideration of award. Offerors must submit each item of quote in a separate electronic attachment clearly identified as described below. Offerors are responsible for including sufficient details to permit a complete and accurate evaluation of each quote.

DISCUSSIONS: The Government intends to award without discussions but reserves the right to conduct discussion should it be deemed in the Government's best interest.

VOLUME 1:
Section A Total price will be evaluated by the Government. Proposed price should reflect a clear understanding of the requirements, is consistent with the various elements of the offer.  

VOLUME 2 
Section A Technical Proposal: Offeror shall submit company's detailed technical approach and method

Instructor-led, in-person instruction

Location:

Colin A. Bird

Price

opps.title: Video Productions and Audiovisual Support for Projects - Joint Task Force-National Capitol Region
opps.description:
The Mission and Installation Contracting Command (MICC) Center – Fort Knox has a requirement from Production Acquisition Division (PAD), Joint Task Force-National Capital Region (JTF-NCR)/Military District Washington (MDW), J/G-42 Production Acquisition Division (PAD) to provide timely quality Video Productions and Audiovisual Support for Projects. The projects will support requirements generated by Army units, as well as other DoW and federal government agencies.

The vast majority of video productions are created to address specific training, information and/or communication objectives as identified by the requiring organization. Products are intended for different audiences ranging from senior DoW leadership and Congressional staff, to military servicemembers and their families, non-governmental organizations, community groups and the general public. Details of specific objectives and target audiences will be contained within the PWS of each individual task order isued under the Multi-Award Task Order Contracts (MATOC).

This RFP seeks to establish the initial pool of unpriced MATOCs on the Indefinate Delivery Indefnite Quantity (IDIQ) basis, with an ordering period of five (5) years. 

The Request for Proposal (RFP) and all attachments are attached and published with this notice.

*****************************************************************************************

Notice is amended to provide Government responses to Industry submitted questions and revised documents resulting from those answers.

Offerors are reminded they are responsible for acknowledging all amendments by including a signed copy of the SF-30 with their proposal submission. Failure to acknowledge an amendment may render the offeror’s overall proposal nonresponsive and ineligible for award.

     Government Responses to Industry Questions

opps.title: RFI/SSN for “Protecting Americans from Tobacco-Related Death and Disease” support services.
opps.description:
The U.S. Food and Drug Administration's (FDA), Office of Acquisitions and Grants Services (OAGS), is conducting market research for “Protecting Americans from Tobacco-Related Death and Disease” support services.

The purpose of this contract is to provide CTP with technical services to support a full range of public education activities. Such activities include planning, developing, producing, and delivering public education campaigns or programs that effectively reach intended audiences using proven and privacy-safe marketing tools. Further specifications will be defined at the Task Order level.

The Contractor shall provide comprehensive services for multiple public education campaigns targeting audiences as small as 10,000 people to as large as 100 million people, to include, but not limited to, financing and placing annual media buys up to or in excess of $100 million. CTP may also direct the Contractor to develop and implement additional public education campaigns, efforts, or activities to support other goals related to its regulatory authorities under the TCA.

The Contractor’s management responsibilities shall include all activities necessary to ensure the accomplishment of timely and effective support. The contract is inclusive of labor and Other Direct Costs (ODCs), both of which will be funded annually throughout the period of performance.

This is not a solicitation announcement. This RFI/SSN is being issued in accordance with Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) Part 10, Market Research. It is for information, planning and market research purposes only and is not to be construed as a commitment by the Government to issue a solicitation or ultimately award a contract. This notice does not obligate the Government to award a contract or otherwise pay for the information provided in response. Respondents are advised that the Government is under no obligation to acknowledge receipt of the information received or provide feedback to resp

opps.description:
This is a notice of intent to solicit and award a sole source contract in accordance with the acquisition of commercial products and commercial services under the simplified acquisition threshold in accordance with Revolutionary FAR Overhaul (RFO) Subpart 12.102, Restricting Competition.  

The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Office of Procurement Operations (OPO) on behalf of the DHS Science and Technology Directorate (S&T) hereby issues this notice of intent to award a sole source contract to Quorum Analytics, LLC, 1 Thomas Circle, NW, 6th Floor, Washington, DC 20005.

 Background

To fulfill its mission, the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Science and Technology Directorate (S&T) requires access to specialized scientific journals and databases to stay current with emerging research and developments. Quorum is a leading platform for federal bill tracking, social media tracking, and a legislative engagement platform. By acquiring Quorum, DHS S&T staff will gain real-time access to government data, monitor legislative activity, and conduct comprehensive policy analysis. This capability will strengthen operational effectiveness, support informed decision-making, and streamline research efforts, ultimately advancing mission-critical objectives. S&T will procure five (5)  Quorum subscriptions for (5) users.

Quorum is the sole provider of an integrated platform that offers real-time federal bill tracking, social media monitoring, and legislative engagement capabilities. No other vendor delivers this unique combination of services with the same breadth, depth, and real-time functionality.

Quorum is uniquely positioned to meet DHS S&T’s needs for the following reasons:

Exclusive Integrated Platform: Quorum is the only platform that combines federal bill tracking, social media tracking, and legislative engagement in a single, unified system. This integration is not available from any other vendor.

Real-Time Access: Quorum provides

opps.title: HHS AI Power User Advanced Models and Features Pilot
opps.agency: HHS Office of the Assistant Secretary for Administration
opps.description:
STATEMENT OF OBJECTIVES



HHS AI Power User Advanced Models and Features Pilot



June 26, 2026



1. Purpose

The purpose of this acquisition is to obtain short-duration, firm-fixed-price pilot awards that function as inclusive, all-you-can-eat-style access bundles, within a stated usage envelope, for up to [1,000] authorized portable HHS power users per resultant award, with potential priced options to increase the authorized portable-user quantity up to 10,000. The objective is to let power users exercise advanced models, advanced features, integration paths, native apps, reporting functions, administrative controls, and security-boundary options in a forward-leaning enterprise environment before HHS finalizes its broader enterprise AI solution.



The primary purpose of the advanced power-user pilot is to establish operational baselines for emerging and frontier AI capabilities  to enable HHS to accurately forecast enterprise demand, define enterprise operating models, establish governance requirements, determine enterprise scalability, or develop future pricing structures.



HHS also intends to mature enterprise AI governance, attribution, allocation, and consumption measurement capabilities throughout the pilot period, including support for capability-level attribution, program-level allocation, enterprise reporting, and AI Consumption Unit (ACU) normalization methodologies.



The pilot shall enable the Government to baseline actual power-user usage, determine enterprise-feasible operational methodology, identify and roadmap which models and features require configuration or customization, identify levels and timing of customization, establish security and authorization logic, and formulate a common enterprise logical and operational model for AI use at HHS.



HHS requires the pilot to establish a practical operating model for commercial-parity access to advanced AI models and features in a forward-leaning enterprise environment. The contractor shall suppo
opps.summary:

 The Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) is initiating an Advanced AI Power User Pilot to evaluate, baseline, and operationalize advanced commercial artificial intelligence (AI) capabilities. This pilot aims to support future enterprise-wide adoption and acquisition decisions by providing practical experience with advanced large language model (LLM) technologies, advanced features, agentic capabilities, APIs, administrative controls, reporting tools, and integration pathways within a secure enterprise environment.

Additionally, there is no obligation on the part of the Government to acquire any products or services described in this RFI.  Your response to this RFI will be treated only as information for the Government to consider.  You will not be entitled to payment for direct or indirect costs that you incur in responding to this RFI.  This request does not constitute a solicitation for proposals or the authority to enter into negotiations to award a task order.  No funds have been authorized, appropriated or received for this effort.  The information provided may be used by the VA in developing its acquisition strategy.  Interested parties are responsible for adequately marking proprietary, restricted or competition sensitive information contained in their response.  The Government does not intend to pay for the information submitted in response to this RFI. 

2. Submittal Information:

All responsible sources may submit a response in accordance with the below information.  

There is a page limitation for this RFI of 10-pages.  The Government will not review any other information or attachments included, that are in excess of the 10-page limit. NO MARKETING MATERIALS ARE ALLOWED AS PART OF THIS RFI.  Generic capability statements will not be accepted or reviewed. The Government requests that it not be inundated with artifacts and peripheral content, and that the submission is readable. Your response must address capabilities specific to the services required in the attached draft Performance Work Statement (PWS) and must include the following:

Interested Vendors shall at a minimum, provide the following information in the initial paragraph of the submission:

The Internal Revenue Service (IRS) anticipates awarding a Firm Fixed Price contract for, Oracle Exadata Hardware, Software, and Maintenance. NAICS Code: 541512 - Computer System Design
OBJECTIVE
The Internal Revenue Service (IRS) relies on Oracle Exadata platforms to support enterprise-wide database services that underpin mission-critical tax administration, modernization, and operational systems. The existing Exadata X8 infrastructure is approaching end of useful life and no longer meets IRS infrastructure currency standards, lifecycle support requirements, or forecasted workload demands. As a result, the IRS must execute a technology refresh to maintain compliant, supportable, and scalable database hosting capabilities. Please refer to the Statement of Work (SOW) for requirement specifications.

All task orders issued against this IDIQ will be Firm Fixed Price. Award will be made based on the best value to the Government using a subjective tradeoff source selection process. 



Please note all RFP amendments or other publicly releasable information will reside within this announcement in the Contract Opportunities section of SAM.gov.

Pohlman, Contracting Officer at email: Douglas.E.Pohlman@usace.army.mil.



The Government will consider all correspondence submitted prior to the deadline for questions, but will not issue individual responses. H9126926IJ35S



Title: GFM Decision-Ready Data Products (Gold Tables) Services in the SIPRNet War Data Platform

Managed By: OSW Chief Data and Artificial Intelligence Office (CDAO)



Environment: SIPRNet War Data Platform (WDP)



This is a Request for Information (RFI) to industry, academia, and the federal lab consortium for feedback. It is a Request for Information supporting Market Research, not a Request for Quote (RFQ).



Joint Staff J35 South is looking for feedback on what technical approach respondents would take or suggest, any suggested modification(s) to the Performance Work Statement (PWS), and a rough order magnitude (ROM) cost estimate for the work to be done across the FYDP.  This is open to as much of a technology (data, artificial intelligence, GenAI, Predictive AI, machine learning) base as possible.



In addition, responses to this posting should provide identifying information such as organization name, address, business size, CAGE Code or UEID, and a point of contact, telephone number, facsimile number, email address, etc.  Interested firms are encouraged to also provide a description of services they can provide, a summary of their company’s capabilities including description of facilities, personnel, and past experience.



Suspense: 15 July 2026



Respond to: Shawn Francis, Joint Staff J35 South, shawn.p.francis2.civ@mail.mil

This announcement is issued solely for information and planning purposes.



It does not constitute a solicitation (Request for Proposal or Request for Quotations) or a promise to issue a solici

opps.title: International Procurement Experts
opps.description:
The Millennium Challenge Corporation (MCC), Program Acquisition & Assistance Practice Group (PAA) within the Department of Compact Operations (DCO) seeks to award contract with a firm to provide multiple procurement experts on an as-needed basis with extensive international procurement experience under donor funded projects, such as funded by the World Bank, MCC, Asian Development Bank etc. These experts will support MCC PAA in developing procurement policy, guidance, and tools for use by MCC partner country Accountable Entities (MCAs) in their procurement operations and assist MCC PAA in measuring performance of those operations and recommending improvements in procedures and application of tools. Extensive interaction with MCC will be required for the experts to fully understand MCC's procurement documents and/or to advise MCC in modifying these documents as and when needed.

See Section M of the attached solicitation for evaluation criteria.

03/19/2026: FA4452-26-S-C001 AOI (1) - AMC/A6 Information Technology Needs Commercial Solutions Opening (CSO) - Amendment 01


	All details regarding submission and evaluation factors are provided in any posted Areas of Interest (AOIs)


This CSO Solicitation is a competitive solicitation that is open until 26 February 2031. Submissions will only be accepted following specific AOI submission due dates and times. The Government reserves the right to extend this CSO beyond the closed date by amendment if the authority is extended.

*White Paper due date for the Base IT Modernization AOI is extended to 12 May 2026.
opps.summary:

opps.title: Security Operation Analytics Rigor (SOAR)
opps.description:
***5/28/26 - updated set-aside, Solicitation Release Date, and Period of Performanace***

The U.S. Citizenship & Immigration Services (USCIS) has a requirement for Security Operation Analytics Rigor (SOAR), consisting of teams to provide development, security, operations (DevSecOps), data processing and analytic services to support USCIS, Information Technology (IT) development delivery services. These services primarily provide identification of Fraud, National Security and Public Safety concerns. The teams focus will be on performing development, security, and operational support activities. The team will also perform, enhancements and refinements of algorithms as well as standardizing large-scale, complex, internal and external applications. 

All applications in the portfolio employ a baseline set of DevSecOps development postures along with USCIS required development gates, safeguards and requirements. They can be categorized into several overarching categories: Case Management, Investigation, Data Storage / Operations, Screening and Vetting, Immigration Operations, Immigration Law Enforcement, and Intelligence Operations.

*Currently performing market research*

BLN24 gap — no confirmed prime cybersecurity delivery; would need dedicated cyber partner

opps.description:
The Contractor shall furnish all labor, materials, supervision, transportation, equipment, and supplies necessary to perform comprehensive bird surveys at the Red Slough Wildlife Management Area of the Ouachita National Forest, Oklahoma. The Contractor shall perform all work in accordance with the specifications detailed in the attachments. Expertise in identifying, by sight and sound, all bird species occurring in Southeast Oklahoma required.

It is anticipated that a firm-fixed price contract consisting of one (1) one-year base period and four (4) one-year option periods shall be awarded as a result of this synopsis/solicitation. The total duration of this contract shall not exceed five (5) years.

The Government intends to evaluate quotations using a comparative evaluation methodology.
opps.summary:

 The Red Slough Wildlife Management Area is a 5,814-acre wetland project designed to restore hydrology and re-establish bottomland hardwoods. It is cooperatively managed by the U.S.D.A. The project aims to maximize biodiversity and provide outstanding recreational opportunities, making it a popular destination for birdwatching and waterfowl hunting.

The purpose of this survey is to monitor the effects of management practices on bird populations in this area.

opps.title: BPA for Shared Electronic Health Record (EHR) Platform and Support Services
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Other Computer Services (Census DWS lane)
opps.description:
U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), under the direction of the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), will issue and manage a Blanket Purchase Agreement (BPA) for an integrated, multi-module correctional Electronic Health Record (EHR) system. Under this BPA, two task orders will be issued: one for ICE Health Service Corps (IHSC) and one for U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP). 

The Contractor shall deliver an integrated, multi-module correctional EHR system that enables secure, interoperable, and longitudinal health records across components, while supporting component-specific operational workflows. The system shall interface seamlessly with DHS enterprise services and external components and stakeholder systems to support evidence-based care, patient safety, and continuity of care. The EHR shall maintain a single, longitudinal health record per detainee across custody transitions (e.g., CBP to IHSC), ensuring data integrity, traceability, and real-time accessibility, in compliance with all applicable federal, DHS, and component-level policies and standards.

The vendor must assess whether the proposed solution provides a holistic, integrated system encompassing all functionalities of the current EHR subsystems. If the proposed system cannot deliver all required functionalities to all facilities covered under each task order by the intended deployment date, the vendor shall identify and engage qualified subcontractors as necessary to address any gaps and ensure all requirements are met.

The proposed solution must be certified by the Office of the National Coordinator for Health Information Technology (ONC-HIT). If not already ONC-HIT certified, the vendor shall obtain certification within one year of implementation. The solution must be FedRAMP high authorized or able to demonstrate an equivalent security certification. The solutions must run on ICE GovCloud or receive an ICE Chief Information Officer exception to be hosted in an alternate FedRAM

The Marine Corps Installations Command (MCICOM) Headquarters (HQ) Contracting Office, located in Arlington, VA, intends to negotiate and award a sole-source modification to increase the shared maximum ordering ceiling of the MCICOM Professional Services Support (PSS) Multiple Award Contract (MAC) Lot 1 (Unrestricted). The affected Indefinite-Delivery/Indefinite-Quantity (IDIQ) contracts are:




	M95494-25-D-5001 (Booz Allen Hamilton Inc)
	M95494-25-D-5002 (Concurrent Technologies Corp)
	M95494-25-D-5003 (KPMG LLP)




Description of Action:


The Government intends to increase the Lot 1 shared maximum ordering ceiling by $96,718,238.12 from $101,427,376.71 to $198,145,614.83. This modification is necessary to address an unanticipated surge in high-priority, in-scope requirements driven by new Department of War (DoW) mandates, including the 2030 Barracks Initiative, enterprise-wide Facility-Related Control Systems (FRCS) cybersecurity, and other needs identified in the Future Years Defense Program (FYDP). This action increases only the contract ceiling capacity and does not extend the current ordering period, which concludes on 5 November 2029, nor does it alter the scope of the base contracts.



Statutory Authority:


This action will be executed on a basis of other than full and open competition (via a class Justification & Approval) pursuant to 10 U.S.C. § 3204(a)(1), as implemented by FAR 6.103-1: Only one responsible source and no other supplies or services will satisfy agency requirements. The Government has determined that the existing Lot 1 prime contractors are the only sources capable of immediately absorbing this surge in critical requirements without causing unacceptable delays and substantial duplication of costs.



Justification Rationale:


A new competitive action to fulfill these urgent requirements would result in an estimated procurement delay of 12-18 months, which would severely disrupt ongoing MCICOM operations and hinder the executi
win_reasons: Full & Open — competing against all firm sizes; lower win probability without incumbent advantage
No direct capability lane match found — scope may not align with BLN24 proven delivery
⚠️ Scope gap: Cybersecurity — BLN24 has no confirmed prime delivery in these lanes
opps.vehicle_note: Contract VEHICLE (IDIQ/GWAC/on-ramp): award is by self-scoring qualification thresholds, not head-to-head competition. Win-prob %, competitor lists, and listed value are not meaningful here — the real question is whether BLN24 clears the domain scorecard.

tasks.note: BLN24 gap — no confirmed prime cybersecurity delivery; would need dedicated cyber partner

capability_matrix.fit_reason: All identified task areas fall outside BLN24's proven delivery lanes

qualifying.q: Do we have the capabilities needed?

qualifying.answer: $96.7M — Tier 2+ (BLN24 has won at this range)

opps.title: Command Post Telecommunications System Upgrade
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Custom Computer Programming (web/software — MBDA/Census lane)
opps.description:
Purpose of RFI
This Request for Information (RFI) is issued solely for market research and acquisition planning purposes. The Government is seeking to identify potential sources capable of providing telecommunication upgrades in accordance with the attached Performance Work Statement. A primary objective of this RFI is to solicit industry feedback to shape the acquisition strategy, validate the use of specific contract vehicles, and confirm the appropriate industry classification for this effort.

NAICS Code Verification
Based on preliminary market research, the Government anticipates classifying this requirement under NAICS code 541519. Industry partners are requested to review the draft requirements and provide feedback on whether this NAICS code accurately reflects the primary scope of work. If a respondent believes an alternative NAICS code is more appropriate, please provide the recommended code along with a brief justification.

No Contract Award: This RFI is issued solely for information and planning purposes. It does not constitute a Request for Proposal (RFP), Request for Quote (RFQ), or Invitation for Bid (IFB), and it shall not be construed as a commitment by the Government to mutually agree to or contract for any services. The Government is not seeking proposals at this time and will not accept unsolicited proposals in response to this notice.

No Reimbursement of Costs: The Government will not pay or reimburse respondents for any costs, effort, or travel associated with preparing or submitting responses to this RFI. All submissions and materials received in response to this RFI will become the property of the United States Government and will not be returned.

Future Acquisitions: A determination by the Government not to compete this proposed effort on a full and open competition basis, or to utilize a specific contract vehicle based upon responses to this notice, is solely within the discretion of the Government.

opps.title: TE/GE Employee Plans AI Assistor RFI
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Other Computer Services (Census DWS lane)
opps.description:
REQUEST FOR INFORMATION (RFI) – TE/GE Employee Plans AI Assistor

The Internal Revenue Service (IRS), Tax Exempt and Government Entities (TE/GE), Employee Plans (EP) Division, is conducting market research to identify commercially available and emerging Artificial Intelligence (AI) solutions that can enhance retirement plan compliance activities, improve operational efficiency, strengthen compliance risk identification, and support informed decision-making by IRS personnel.

The Government seeks information on AI-enabled solutions capable of securely ingesting and analyzing structured and unstructured retirement plan data; interpreting plan documents and applicable statutory and regulatory requirements; performing retirement plan calculations and compliance analyses; generating reports and workpapers; conducting scenario modeling; integrating with enterprise systems such as PegaSystems and Salesforce; and providing secure, explainable, and governed AI outputs.

THIS IS A REQUEST FOR INFORMATION (RFI) ONLY. This notice is issued solely for market research purposes in accordance with FAR Part 10 and FAR 15.201. It does not constitute a solicitation, request for proposals, or request for quotations. The Government will not reimburse respondents for any costs associated with responding.

Responses will be used to assess marketplace capabilities, identify potential sources, support acquisition planning, develop an Independent Government Cost Estimate (IGCE), evaluate commercial practices, and inform future procurement strategy. There is no funding currently associated with this RFI.

Interested vendors are invited to submit a capability statement and responses to the attached RFI. Responses shall not exceed 16 pages total (15 pages plus a one-page cover sheet) and must be submitted electronically in Microsoft Word or PDF format.

Questions are due no later than 12:00 p.m. Eastern Time on July 2, 2026.

RFI responses are due no later than 12:00 p.m. Eastern Time on

opps.title: Notification of Intent to Award a Sole Source Purchase Order to LexisNexis
opps.description:
This notice is being published in accordance with Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) Subpart 5.101 requiring dissemination of information for proposed contract actions. This is a notice of intent to award a sole source purchase order. THIS NOTICE IS NOT A REQUEST FOR COMPETITIVE PROPOSALS.

The Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) intends to negotiate and award a sole-source purchase order to LexisNexis for StateNet. The Government intends to procure this requirement in accordance with Revolutionary FAR Overhaul (RFO) Part 12, specifically RFO FAR 12.102(a), for acquisitions valued at or below the simplified acquisition threshold (SAT).

The period of performance for this requirement is a Base year and four (4) 1-year Option years.

The CFTC requires a tool to track state legislation concerning prediction markets with real-time alerts and forecasting capabilities. StateNet provides comprehensive coverage of legislation, regulations, and policy activity across all 50 states, Congress, and key U.S. territories. StateNet provides access to bills, rules, statutes, executive orders, legislative hearings, regulatory notices, and rulemaking activity. The CFTC is unaware of any alternative solutions that can meet the agency’s requirements.

This notice is not a competitive request for proposals.  A competitive solicitation will not be issued. Interested parties who believe they can provide these services may submit a capabilities statement to the Contracting Officer no later than July 8, 2026, at 10:00 AM Eastern Time. Responses must be submitted via email. Information received will be considered solely for the purpose of Market Research. A determination by the Government not to compete this proposed contract based upon responses to this notice is solely within the discretion of the Government.

The Government will not be responsible for any costs of preparation in response to this announcement.

opps.title: 2030 Decennial Administrative Recruiting, Hiring, and Training System (DARHTS)
opps.description:
The Census Integrated Personnel and Payroll System (CIPPS) is an integrated suite of government and vendor systems for recruiting, assessing, fingerprinting, background clearance, hiring, onboarding, benefits, and training temporary employees* in support of the 2028 Dress Rehearsal and 2030 Decennial Census. 

The purpose of this requirement is to obtain technical contractor services for the 2030 Decennial Administrative, Recruiting, Hiring, and Training System (DARHTS). The 2030 DARHTS is the primary Census system for automating, standardizing, and streamlining of job applications and assessments during the 2028 Dress Rehearsal and 2030 Census. This requirement will also obtain a robust, integrated training solution and Help Desk (optional) operations during the 2028 Dress Rehearsal and 2030 Census. The provided training solution shall utilize a FedRAMP (Moderate or Higher) compliant Software as a Service (SaaS) solution.

Note that the Solicitation along with Attachments 1 through 3 and 5 through 14 are attached. Attachment 4 is not included with this solicitation and will be provided after award.

Note that this is a Hybrid Type Contract with Firm Fixed Price (FFP) components as well as Time and Material (T&M) components.

Concise Description of Services

PBGC, a wholly owned U.S Government corporation, administers the pension plan termination insurance program established by Title IV of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974, as amended. PBGC protects the retirement income of approximately 30 million American workers and beneficiaries in both single-employer and multiemployer private sector pension plans.

PBGC’s Corporate Finance and Restructuring Department requires the assistance of an experienced financial advisory services firm with proven experience in the U.S. information technology (IT) sector leading large distress valuation and turnaround engagements and/or representing clients in a lead capacity on large and complex restructuring and/or bankruptcy transactions involving enterprises with funded debt exceeding $4 billion. PBGC will place particularly high value on prior experience with the PBGC pension termination process and on contractor personnel with relevant expert testimony experience.

FNA is seeking a contractor to provide expert technical assistance to States to expand and improve their SNAP E&T programs. -	Develop and disseminate the SNAP E&T Review, a periodic digest of SNAP E&T relevant events, announcements and other news relevant to SNAP E&T.

opps.description:
American Battle Monuments Commission (ABMC) is issuing this Request for Information (RFI) for market research purposes. ABMC seeks industry input on cloud based digital asset management platforms and transition approaches that can enhance or replace the current platform at WeRemember.ABMC.gov and support long term stewardship of burial and memorialization records, digital assets, and public access services.

ABMC is particularly interested in digital asset management platforms that are proven, support federal requirements, and can be configured without excessive custom development such as (in alphabetical order): Alfresco, Aprimo, ArchivesSpace, AtoM, Axiell DAMS powered by Piction, Bynder, Canto, Hyland, Nuxeo, OpenText, Preservica, ResourceSpace, and comparable digital asset management or archival platforms

opps.agency: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency
opps.description:
The Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) hereby announces its intent to solicit and enter into a sole source contract with FactSet Research System, Inc 45 Glover Ave, Suite 700, FL 7, Norwalk, CT 06850, in accordance with FAR RFO 12.102 (a) only one source is reasonably available valued at or below the simplified acquisition threshold.  This notice complies with FAR RFO 5.101 pre-solicitation notice.

The OCC requires an enterprise license subscription to access Committee on Uniform Security Identification Procedures (CUSIP).  A CUSIP is a nine-digit number or nine-character alphanumeric code that identifies a North American financial instrument, including stocks and bonds, facilitating trading, clearance and settlement. CUSIP is managed by FactSet Research Systems, Inc on behalf of the American Bankers Association. The license enables the OCC to use CUSIPs embedded within OC data products and third-party data products for OCC related research and analytical efforts.  The base period of performance is 09.30.26 – 09.29.27, with four 12-month option periods.

This is not a request for competitive quotes; however, the Government will consider responses from all responsible sources received no later than 12:00 PM Eastern Time on July 10, 2026. Responses must be supported with clear and convincing evidence to clearly be able to provide the services outlined in this notice. Any responses received will only be considered for the purpose of determining whether to conduct a competitive procurement in lieu of the sole source described in this notice, and such determination shall be made at the sole discretion of the OCC. 

Responses must be submitted in writing to Danielle Pena via email at Danielle.pena@occ.treas.gov, no later than the timeframe noted above.  Telephone responses and requests will not be received.

Background and Introduction

Joint Clothing Textile Modernization Initiative (JCTMI) is a United States (US) Army Product Manager (PdM) Soldier Clothing and Individual Equipment (SCIE) effort to fundamentally modernize the acquisition of Clothing and Textile (C&T) items through the adoption of Apparel Tech Packs (ATPs) and an apparel Commercial off the Shelf (COTS) Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) software system. This initiative aligns with Department of War (DoW) executive directives and Secretary of War (SECWAR) priorities to modernize acquisition processes, strengthen the defense industrial base, reduce barriers to entry for industry partners, and build supply chain resiliency to meet operational readiness and force modernization goals.

opps.summary:

opps.title: System Advisor Model (SAM) Software Development
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Custom Computer Programming (web/software — MBDA/Census lane)
opps.description:
This is a Sources Sought Notice (SSN) issued by the Alliance for Energy Innovation, LLC (Alliance). The purpose of this SSN is to conduct market research and gather information on the current capabilities of potential sources to provide support, maintenance, and expansion of the System Advisor Model (SAM).

The National Laboratory of the Rockies (NLR) develops and maintains SAM, an open-source software platform used by researchers, industry, utilities, and government organizations to evaluate the technical and economic performance of renewable energy technologies. SAM supports a wide range of energy technologies, including solar, wind, and geothermal systems, and provides performance, financial, and incentive modeling capabilities.

This effort includes software architecture improvements, implementation of new modeling capabilities, user interface enhancements, cross-platform software support, and troubleshooting of issues identified by users and development staff. The work supports annual software releases and ongoing modernization of the platform.

This is a SOURCES SOUGHT NOTICE to determine the capabilities of companies. This notice does not constitute a solicitation announcement, nor does it restrict the Government to an ultimate acquisition approach. The Government will use the responses to this notice to make acquisition decisions.

Pittsburgh District, US Army Corps of Engineers, has a requirement for necessary technical support, software maintenance, and troubleshooting resources to maintain the reliability, security, and operational readiness of the organization's industrial automation control systems and Rockwell Automation hardware and software.

The Contractor shall provide comprehensive TechConnect support that encompasses two specific tiers of service: Self-Assist Support and Product Support.
