The support will cover the designated inventory of programmable logic controllers (PLCs), human-machine interfaces (HMIs), drives, and associated Rockwell Automation software.

A draft copy of the Performance Work Statement is attached to this notice.

The submission of a capabilities statement is limited to 30 pages and must include:

1) Company name, UEI/CAGE, address, point of contact, and contact information.

2) Company interest in providing a quote for a solicitation, and the capability to perform.

opps.title: HITU Enterprise Health IT Staffing and Professional Services Support
opps.description:
The Department of Homeland Security, Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), Enforcement and Removal Operations (ERO), ICE Health Services Corps (IHSC) seeks to procure professional staffing services to support the Health Information and Technology Unit (HITU). The contractor will provide qualified personnel to assist with the decommissioning of the current electronic health record (EHR) system and the development, deployment, and management of a modernized EHR for IHSC and Customs and Border Protection (CBP) facilities nationwide. Services include, but are not limited to, business and operational support, data analytics, project management, technical writing, software engineering, information security, training, and solutions development. The contract covers both baseline staffing and optional surge support to address fluctuating workloads and emergent priorities. All personnel must meet the education, experience, and certification requirements specified in the PWS and its appendices.

Key positions include Program Manager, Health IT Data Engineer, Data Scientist, Data Analyst, Software Engineer, Data Strategist, Management Analyst, Technical Writer, Reporting Analyst, Business Analyst, Clinical Informaticist, Information Systems Security Officer, Technical Engineer, Training Specialist, Project Manager, Solutions Developer, and Systems Integrator. 

The contractor will ensure continuity of support, compliance with government standards, and provide key personnel as designated by the government.

opps.title: Video Teleconferencing Modernization
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Other Computer Services (Census DWS lane)
opps.description:
A.T. Augusta Military Medical Center (ATAMMC), Information Management/Information Technology (IM/IT) Department has a requirement for modernizing the VTC Systems throughout the hospital.

VTC Technology modernization is required to increase overall collaboration efficiencies, and to reduce room downtime. The hospital is interested in implementing standards-based collaboration VTC systems that can be deployed throughout the hospital, taking into consideration the unique requirements of any given collaboration space. Building standards will promote quicker user adoption and lessen the burden on administrative staff who are required to maintain and support these spaces.  Contractor shall provide warranty/maintenance for the ATAMMC throughout the period of 36 months.  Contractor shall also make the best effort to minimize new holes necessary for cable installation.

The vendor shall provide new VTC system materials listed in SOW for ATAMMC that includes the standard equipment warranty for 36 months. All maintenance shall include technical support, patches, updates, and upgrades on all work products.

The address for the facility:

A.T. Augusta Military Medical Center

9300 DeWitt Loop, 

Ft. Belvoir, VA 22060



Points of Contact for Facility Access:

Ms. Donna Swan

PH: 571-231-0499

Cell: 571-460-7220

Email: donna.d.swan2.civ@health.mil

Mr. David Chase

PH: 571-231-0381

Cell: 571-992-3629

Email: david.r.chase.civ@health.mil

All vendors must be registered in SAM.gov AND registration must be valid in order to submit a proposal. If your company’s registration has run out or is on the verge of expiring, please update your registration, preferably before the site visit. An expired registration on SAM.gov doesn’t stop you from participating in the site visit, however, it prevents you from submitting a formal proposal for this requirement and therefore your proposal will not be considered.

A Base Access Form will need to be filled out and submitted by each

opps.description:
This is a combined synopsis/solicitation for commercial products or commercial services prepared in accordance with part 12. This announcement constitutes the only solicitation. Offers are being requested and a separate written solicitation will not be issued.

Solicitation number 337372 is issued as a request for quotation (RFQ) for a consolidated, cloud-based Commercial-Off-The-Shelf (COTS) Freshworks enterprise service management (ESM) platform, or equal, to replace its existing ITSM solution and implement standardized customer and enterprise case management capabilities across the agency. 

The solution must support approximately 550 internal users and designated agents, public media and historical service inquiries, and enable consistent intake, routing, Service Level Agreement (SLA) management, reporting, automation, and role-based access control. The platform must integrate with Microsoft 365 E5 and Microsoft Entra ID, support secure public-facing intake where required, and provide enterprise-grade auditability and data governance. 

This requirement is issued on a brand name, or equal, basis for the Freshworks platform. ABMC has determined that limiting competition is appropriate and the supporting rationale is documented and maintained in the contract file.. See attached Performance Work Statement for complete details.

reference. The full text of provisions and clauses may be accessed electronically at www.acquisition.gov.

opps.title: Air Force Criminal Justice Information Center (AFCJIC)
opps.description: Air Forice Office of Special Investigation (AFOSI) has a requirement for  The Air Force Criminal Justice Information Center (AFCJIC) integrates and audits criminal history records from law enforcement and judge advocate professionals to deliver accurate and timely criminal history records to external stakeholders ensuring public safety and the rights of the individual.  The organization is aligned under the commander of the DAF Office of Special Investigations (OSI) and serves as the primary focal point for the exchange of criminal justice information between DAF entities and US and foreign government criminal justice jurisdictions.  This contract requirement increases capacity of a training team, an audit team, an operations team, and a mission support team.  The Contractor shall interface and perform technical and programmatic liaison support across the DAF-CJIC and with DAF Headquarters elements conducting or interfacing with indexing or criminal justice information management requirements, operations, and planning.  The Contractor shall provide support to perform information and records management utilizing multiple government databases to request, receive, inventory, and prepare information for entry into criminal justice databases internal and external to the DAF.  Additionally, the Contractor shall support related administrative functions in support of personnel assigned to the DAF-CJIC, various DAF entities that accomplish indexing operations, and/or indexing task forces IAW the PWS.

REQUEST FOR INFORMATION FOR MARKET RESEARCH PURPOSES

SOF Enterprise Professional Services (SEPS) II

2 July 2026

NOTICE: This notice is being issued for market research purposes only.  The information obtained as a result of this notice will be used to assist the Government in its acquisition strategy. 

This Request for Information (RFI) is for Market Research purposes only and must not be considered as an invitation for bid, request for quotation, request for proposal, or as an obligation on the part of the Government to acquire any products or services.  In accordance with FAR 15.101(c)(2), responses to this notice are not offers and cannot be accepted by the Government to form a binding contract. No contract awards will be made from this notice, and no solicitation is available at this time. No entitlement to payment of direct or indirect costs or charges by the Government will arise as a result of contractor submission of responses to this announcement or the Government use of such information.

The acquisition of services will be in support of the SOF Enterprise Professional Services (SEPS) II acquisition, which will focus on acquiring Knowledge Based Services, as defined by Department of Defense taxonomy for the acquisition of services. The Government is contemplating this acquisition as a Service-Disabled Veteran Owned Small Business (SDVOSB) Set-aside.  A USSOCOM single award IDIQ contract for current similar services, SOF Enterprise Professional Services (SEPS) will be ending May 16, 2027.

NOTE: IAW FAR 9.5, “Organizational Conflict of Interest” (OCI) compliance is a material requirement of this contract. opps.title: FEMA seeks a partnership with a Contractor to develop a database on a Microsoft Access planform that provides the unit cost for FEMA home repair components.
opps.description: FEMA seeks a partnership with a Contractor to develop a database on a Microsoft Access platform that provides the unit cost for FEMA home repair components. The Contractor shall perform research of repair line-item costs in each Federal Information Processing Standards (FIPS) code areas across the country to provide a database that represents the local economy in a disaster location. Services specified under this contract require quarterly updates and additional pricing work that reflects changes in prices due to current economic conditions in each FIPS code location. Develop and update the FEMA Line-Item Database in a Microsoft Access format with an interactive front-end application capable of entering a disaster number, selecting a state or territory, selecting counties, municipalities or parishes within the state or territory, and exporting the pricing data in FEMAâ€™s designated tab delimited format described in Attachment B. 3.1.1. The line-item format includes a category, line-item number, line-item name, unit of measure and a description of repair represented by the line item. The line-item cost is a sum of all cost components associated in the use of that line item. The database shall be updated quarterly, delivered to FEMA through an FTP (File Transfer Protocol) site to be imported by FEMA. The first deliverable of the base and each option year shall be delivered in the first week of: March=Q1, June=Q2, September=Q3, December=Q4. Develop an annual Line-Item Mapping Master â€“ A Microsoft Excel file of the Line-Item basis and rationale used for pricing that provides a breakdown of the line-item components as CSI codes with national average costs.

National Institute of Justice National Missing and Unidentified Persons System (NIJ-NamUs) REQUEST FOR QUOTATION (RFQ). The goals and objectives of this effort are for the NamUs program to:
(1) operate without interruption,
(2) provide authorization, access, technical support, and training to users of the NamUs databases, and
(3) provide free forensic services to resolve NamUs cases.

The goal of the NamUs program is to work with NIJ to assist federal, state, local and tribal law enforcement, medical examiners and coroners, allied professionals, and the general public with resolving missing, unidentified, and unclaimed persons cases. The scope of this effort is to obtain professional services to provide core elements in support of the NamUs program.

Specific tasks for this effort are detailed in Section 3 of the attached PWS and will be managed by the successful vendor. The successful vendor will work closely with NIJ program scientists and interface with other OJP Bureaus and Offices, as well as the contractors who support them. Services covered within the scope of this effort as well as the specific tasks for this anticipated contract are detailed in Section 3 of the attached PWS include but are not limited to:
TASK AREA 1: Provide Qualified Leadership and Staff.
TASK AREA 2: Provide Case Management and Investigative Support
TASK AREA 3: Provide Forensic/Biometric Services to assist with Identifying Remains
TASK AREA 4: Communications
TASK AREA 5: Transfer and Transition of NamUs Program
---PLEASE SEE ALL ATTACHMENTS---

opps.description: The U.S. Coast Guard (USCG) Surface Forces Logistics Center (SFLC) seeks an Enhanced Reliability Centered Maintenance (E-RCM) program through the implementation of Natural Language Processing tools include an Artificial Intelligence powered analytic model.  This model shall be pre-trained on Naval ship structure and machinery terminology to identify, extract, index, enhance quality, and analyze unstructured equipment failure reports and technical data from at least the last 5 years.  The model shall be currently utilized to analyze Naval Ship equipment and deployed in a FEDRAMP approved environment. This requirement will also include piloting new automations or prototypes for digitalization use cases. These may include, but are not limited to, tools to promote data driven decision making, data-driven analysis, extracting additional data elements from maintenance documents, more extensive application of natural language processing / large language models, chatbots, developing new automations (RPA or intelligent automation), etc.

opps.title: Mobility Air Forces Electronic Flight Bag (MAF EFB) Support
opps.description:
The 763rd Enterprise Sourcing Squadron (763 ESS) is seeking sources to provide on-going support that provides Headquarters Air Mobility Command Directorate of Strategic Plans, Requirements, & Programs (HQ AMC A5/8) with the workforce necessary to manage Mobility Air Forces Electronic Flight Bag (MAF EFB) operational capabilities and requirements derived from validated user needs.

The 763rd ESS is seeking information for potential sources to provide MAF EFB Support. Headquarters Air Mobility Command’s (HQ AMC) Directorate of Strategic Plans, Requirements, & Programs’ (A5/8) primary responsibility is to oversee tanker and airlift concepts, budget strategies, manpower and organization, competitive sourcing, strategic planning and metrics, programs and policy, operations planning analysis, airlift and tanker doctrine, intergovernmental agreements, congressional interests, and management of the command’s validated acquisition programs.

The EFB is a critical component to aircrew operations. The EFB is an electronic handheld device that enables removal of carry-on paper technical orders/flight manuals and provides ancillary situational awareness to the operator. Over time, the EFB has become critical and necessary equipment for aircrew operations. Since inception, support contractors have been necessary to assist with requirements development and sustainment of the EFB. This PWS is intended to continue necessary contractor support. The contractor shall support HQ AMC/A5R in managing EFB technical requirements and capabilities.

opps.title: IT MANAGER - PERSONAL SERVICES CONTRACTORS
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Other Computer Services (Census DWS lane)
opps.description:
U.S. DEPARTMENT OF THE TREASURY

OFFICE OF TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE



Information Technology Manager



The U.S. Department of the Treasury’s Office of Technical Assistance (OTA) supports the development of strong financial sectors and sound public financial management in countries where assistance is needed and there is a strong commitment to reform. OTA provides technical assistance in over 50 countries in five functional areas: economic crimes, government debt and infrastructure finance, budget and financial accountability, banking and financial services, and revenue administration.



OTA is seeking an Information Technology (IT) Manager to provide a wide range of IT management and administrative services in support of its global projects. The person selected will be hired under a personal services contract. This position is located at OTA headquarters in Washington, DC. This is not a remote position. Some travel may be required.



Qualifications:


	Significant, proven working experience as an IT manager or relevant experience successfully implementing IT strategies and IT security policies, preferably in the public sector
	Technical management experience in computer networks and hardware and software systems
	Expert knowledge of information technology standards and practices to include:
	
		knowledge of IT applications and systems (e.g. operating systems and user interfaces)

opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Other Computer Services (Census DWS lane)
opps.description: SFLC seeks to build upon the Supplier Assurance and Surveillance program to implement a comprehensive Supply Chain Resiliency Program. This program will leverage advanced analytics to monitor and assess supplier performance and manage parts across production, sustainment, and modernization efforts. The goal is to identify, assess, prioritize, and invest in supplier Â­based areas to enhance supply chain resilience and capability. To do this effectively, the USCG requires a configurable commercial-off-the-shelf (COTS) supplier intelligence capability that uses relevant, dynamic analytics and indicators. This program must allow the USCG to monitor and assess risk systematically, and at scale across thousands of suppliers and hundreds of thousands of parts across platforms in various phases of production, sustainment, and modernization. The platform will integrate datasets, provide a robust analytic user interface, and continuously run configurable analytics to aid decision-making. The platform shall be hosted on Contractor-owned hardware and facilities, maintained by the Contractor, and utilize data provided by the Government. Key features of the platform will include: â€¢ A collaborative environment module for cross Â­program communication. â€¢ Configurable analytics and indicators for supply chain risk and obsolescence management. â€¢ Training and support for effective software use.

opps.title: Foundation Sustainment Support, Software and Integration (FS3i)
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Custom Computer Programming (web/software — MBDA/Census lane)
opps.description:
This Notice is associated with the prior FS3i Presoliciation notice under ID: HM0476_20260105 "Foundation Sustainment Support, Software and Integration (FS3i). 

The intent of this notice is to provide offerors access to a "Bidder's Library" supplementing the FS3i acquisition; documents are associated with the draft Request For Proposal (RFP) and upcoming official RFP for the FS3i program.  All RFP documents and attachments will be published in the coming months and will be associated with this notice when published.  

Included in this notice are: 

-Industry Day Event slides

-Final Consolidated FS3i draft solcitation Q&As

-Bidder's Library

The Industry Day attendee list may be provided upon request by email to timothy.j.hull@nga.mil.  

No questions are being requested at this time.    

Additional documents have been added as part of the bidder's library.
opps.summary:
Background
 The National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency (NGA) is issuing this solicitation for the Foundation Sustainment Support, Software and Integration (FS3i) program. The goal of this contract is to provide essential support services that align with the agency's mission to deliver geospatial intelligence and ensure operational readiness.

opps.title: Weather Stream MicroWave Sounder Data Buy
opps.description: This acquisition seeks access to acquire commercial, near-real-time MWS data, including optional hyperspectral observations, from on-orbit MWS systems. The focus is on Low Earth Orbit (LEO) observations suitable for operational data assimilation, short-term forecast improvement, tropical cyclone monitoring, precipitation-related applications, and environmental hazard assessment. Depending on sensor design, MWS may also provide imagery and atmospheric vertical wind profiles. The capabilities described in Section II.B and II.C  will provide NOAA with scalable and flexible access to commercial MW data, enhancing forecast accuracy, situational awareness, and NOAA’s role as the Nation’s authoritative source of environmental intelligence.

***05/15/2026: Estimated solicitation release date updated from 5/11/26 to 7/13/26***

Estimated solicitation release date updated from 4/10/26 to 5/11/26***
***02/03/2026: Estimated solicitation release date updated from 1/19/26 to 4/10/26***
***10/29/2025: Estimated solicitation release date updated from 4/16/26 to 1/19/26; Dollar range updated from Over $100M to $50M-$100M; NAICS Code updated from 541512 to 541519***

USCIS is establishing this record to provide industry with the earliest possible announcement of a major procurement. USCIS has a requirement for Cyber Security Support Services (C3S) II that will utilize GSA HACS 8(a). The C3S-Operations contractor will have a primary responsibility to provide the ability to identify, protect, detect, respond, and recover from cybersecurity threats and incidents. This ability will require highly specialized skill sets with significant experience and the ability to provide ongoing best practices. The limited number of available Government staff with these skill sets make it essential to find commercial sources to supply these services. Commercial sources should also able to provide cutting edge cybersecurity industry best practices. The contracted services will be required for the foreseeable future and a three year period of performance is anticipated.

The successful vendor must demonstrate the necessary skills and in-depth knowledge to ensure the assessment fully complies with federal mandates, including DOC and NOAA policies, and NIST requirements (such as NIST SP 800-37 Rev 2 and NIST SP 800-53A Rev 5). The primary goal is to produce accurate assessment findings that support an Authority to Operate (ATO) recommendation.1

opps.agency: HUD Office of the Chief Procurement Officer
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Custom Computer Programming (web/software — MBDA/Census lane)
opps.description:
The purpose of thsi amendment is to revise the Sources Sought Notice and change the points of contact.

The Department of Housing and Urban Development through the Office of the Chief Information Officer (OCIO) intends to issue a solicitation for Technology Transformation Next Generation (H2TNG) Multiple Award Contract (MAC) Indefinite Delivery/Indefinite Quantity (IDIQ) vehicle. The H2TNG MAC IDIQ is intended to provide comprehensive IT Support Services and Technology Transformation Next Generation (ITSS and 2TNG) solutions across HUD Headquarters, regional and field offices, data centers, and other designated locations, spanning all OCIO functional areas. Through individually awarded Task Orders (TOs), contractors may be required to deliver enterprise-wide capabilities across the IT lifecycle, including governance, acquisition and financial support, enterprise architecture, systems engineering, cloud and data solutions, cybersecurity, IT operations and maintenance, and end user and infrastructure support.

In addition to capability statements being due on Aprl 30, 2026, HUD will be hosting a pre-solicitation conference on April 22, 2026. Pre-registration is required and questions are due by April 16, 2026, not later than 11:00 AM EST. The location of the pre-soliciation will be provded.

opps.description: NOS requires comprehensive support to ensure organizational compliance with Department of Commerce (DOC), NOAA, and FISMA policies by providing technical and non-technical cybersecurity assistance and consultation. The system's primary objective is to manage the NOS cybersecurity posture through the continuous identification of sustainable business process requirements, oversight of security incidents through the NOAA Incident Response and Reporting Application (NIRRA), and management of all organizational data calls. Vendors are expected to provide qualified labor to maintain enterprise-level dashboards, validate user permissions for operational tools such as Tenable Security Center, and provide expert guidance based on NIST Special Publications to improve IT operations and reduce risk. opps.title: Modernization of Logistics and Finance Business Systems (PM LOG‑FIN formerly EBS‑C) – Open and Continuous Call for Solutions (CFS)
opps.description:
This Open and Continuous Call for Solutions (CFS), Notice ID LOGFIN26, is issued under the Army Open Solicitation (AOS) W9128Z‑25‑S‑A002 for the U.S. Army Contracting Command – Rock Island (ACC‑RI). The CFS seeks innovative commercial Low‑Code/No‑Code (LCNC) and modernization solutions to support the Army Logistics and Finance communities through rapid, iterative prototyping aligned to nine Areas of Interest (AOI) representing PM LOG‑FIN Value Chains.

This CFS remains open in perpetuity until canceled. The Government will publish separate Targeted Calls for Solutions (TCFS) under this CFS, each with specific requirements and deadlines. Industry may also submit AOI White Papers at any time identifying capability gaps within the AOIs.

Submissions must address LCNC and modernization capabilities supporting non‑transactional and transactional functions integrating with five current Army ERP systems. Solutions affecting financial transactions must meet DoD auditability, internal controls (ICOFR/ITGC), traceability, and segregation of duties requirements. All solutions must comply with Army cybersecurity, governance, and interoperability standards and be deployable in cArmy 2.0.

The nine Areas of Interest (AOIs) include:
• AOI 01: Mission Operations Support
• AOI 02: Asset Acquisition
• AOI 03: Real Estate
• AOI 04: Supply
• AOI 05: Fulfillment
• AOI 06: Transportation
• AOI 07: Maintenance
• AOI 08: Manufacturing / Remanufacturing
• AOI 09: Resource Allocation

Approved LCNC platforms include Appian, Army Vantage, Palantir, PowerApps, Salesforce, SAP, and ServiceNow.
Submissions must follow all instructions in this announcement. Questions and submissions must be sent to usarmy.ria.acc.mbx.pmlogfin-cso@army.mil. 

The Army Open Solicitation (AOS) that this Call for Solution is associated with can be found at:

https://sam.gov/workspace/contract/opp/09ab570a35484c6cb5c6eba05cad19b3/view

Please review the AOS posting an

opps.title: DCSA INNOVATION GATEWAY
opps.description:
The Defense Counterintelligence and Security Agency (DCSA) Innovation Gateway is a competitive solicitation that will result in the award of prototype projects under Other Transaction Agreements (OTA).

It is our commercially-focused front door for innovators.

Our goal is simple: to partner with creative companies to solve our most critical mission challenges.

Innovation Gateway Process

The Innovation Gateway is a general announcement of DCSA’s general areas of interest (Digital Transformation Enablement, Data Integration and Advanced Analytics, Cyber and Industrial Security, Digital Workforce and Governance). The general soliciation includes criteria for selecting Solution Concept Papers and soliciting the participation of all proposers capable of satisfying the Government’s needs.

Proposal requests will be issued via ‘Innovation Calls’ that are published separately under the DCSA Innovation Gateway at various times during the open period of this solicitation. 

Innovation Calls will be published via Sam.gov through separate solicitation postings referencing the DCSA Innovation Gateway. Proposers are required to use the Solution Concept Paper Template (DCSA Innovation Gateway Attachment 1) to submit their solutions.

The Government will only accept proposals in response to Innovation Calls. Unsolicited proposals that are not in response to an Innovation Call will not be reviewed by the Government.

opps.title: Notice of Intent to Sole Source – SynapseVNA
opps.agency: NIH Office of Logistics and Acquisition Operations
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Other Computer Services (Census DWS lane)
opps.description:
This is a notice of intent, not a request for a quotation. A solicitation document will not be issued and quotations will not be requested.

This procurement is for a one-year (09/04/2026 – 09/03/2027) contract to maintain our current Fuji Medical (formerly TeraMedica)’s SynapseVNA product at the latest revision level and to provide technical support for that product, when necessary.  This package forms the core of the ResearchPACS (Picture Archiving and Communication System) maintained by RADIS/CC on behalf of NIH. Fuji Medical is the developer, sole licensor, and sole provider of technical support for the Synapse family of products. As such, it is in the only entity capable of providing the needed technical updates and support services.

This acquisition is being conducted using policies unique to the Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) Part 13 –Simplified Acquisition Procedures, Subpart 13.106-1(b)(1), For purchases not exceeding the simplified acquisition threshold (SAT), contracting officers may solicit from one source if the contracting officer determines that the circumstance of the contract action deem only one source reasonably available. Contracts awarded using FAR Part 13-Simplified Acquisition Procedures are exempt from the requirements of FAR Part 6-Competition Requirements.

Comments to this announcement, referencing this posting number, may be submitted to the NIH, Clinical Center, Office of Purchasing and Contracts, prior to the closing date specified in this announcement, to the attention of Ryssa Nix, Contract Specialist via email to Ryssa.nix@nih.gov by close date.

NAICS Code: 541519

PSC Code: AN13

Posted Date: 05/07/2026

Close Date: 05/14/2026 12pm EST

End User IC: Clinical Center

POC Name: Ryssa Nix

opps.title: Symbolic Nuclear Analysis Package (SNAP) Development, Maintenance, and Support Follow-On
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Custom Computer Programming (web/software — MBDA/Census lane)
opps.description:
THE U.S. NUCLEAR REGULATORY COMMISSION (NRC) is issuing this Sources Sought Synopsis as a means of conducting market research or as a market survey to determine the availability of potential qualified vendors with the technical capability to provide all management, supervision, administration, and labor for the project titled “Symbolic Nuclear Analysis Package (SNAP) Development, Maintenance, and Support.”

Services are to be provided to the NRC in Rockville, Maryland. The applicable North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code assigned to this procurement is 541511 (Custom Computer Programming Services).

THERE IS NO SOLICITATION AT THIS TIME. This request for sources and vendor information does not constitute a request for proposal; submission of any information in response to this market survey is purely voluntary; the Government assumes no financial responsibility for any costs incurred.

Background and Objective

The U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC), Office of Nuclear Regulatory Research (RES), requires continued development, maintenance, sustainment, and user support for its suite of plugins and plotting tools within the Symbolic Nuclear Analysis Package (SNAP) framework. SNAP is a graphical user interface developed and owned by ISL, Inc. that is licensed to the NRC. The NRC has funded and owns specific plugins within the SNAP framework — including the TRACE, PARCS, MELCOR, MACCS, and FAST plugins — which assist NRC staff and international partners in developing input models and analyzing output for complex nuclear safety codes.

The objective of this follow-on contract is to ensure seamless operation of these NRC-owned plugins, adapt to ongoing updates in the underlying physics codes, provide sustained support for mature plugins, and continue development of GRAVE Plot — an NRC-owned scientific data visualization tool (successor to APTplot) used in conjunction with SNAP workflows.

opps.title: Access Required to Online Real Estate Database Services
opps.description:
This is a combined synopsis/solicitation for commercial items/services prepared in accordance with the format in Federal Acquisition Regulations (FAR) subpart 12.6, “Streamlined Procedures for Evaluation and Solicitation for Commercial Items or Services”, and FAR Part 13 Simplified Acquisition Procedures, as supplemented with additional information included in this notice. This announcement constitutes the only solicitation; quotes are being requested. This solicitation number is 2043FY-26-Q-00038 and is issued as a Request for Quote (RFQ). 

The Internal Revenue Service (IRS) is seeking commercial real estate database subscription services to support the Large Business and International (LB&I) Division's Engineering Program.

The LB&I Division’s Engineering Program provides technical support on engineering and valuation issues involving large and complex business tax returns. Specialists perform work involving commercial real estate appraisal, machinery and equipment valuation, cost segregation studies, and research credit claims.

To support these activities, the Government requires access to a commercial real estate information platform that provides nationwide current and historical market data. The platform must provide comprehensive commercial real estate information, including ownership records, sales transactions, lease information, market trends, and related market analytics necessary to evaluate commercial properties and support tax administration activities. The Government intends to award a contract to the responsible offeror whose quotation conforms to the solicitation requirements and offers the lowest evaluated price. This requirement is unrestricted for full and open competition.

The Statement of Work and all applicable solicitation documents are included as attachments to this notice. Sources who believe they can provide the products/services included in the Statement of Work (SOW) shall submit a price quote via email

opps.title: Master Synch
opps.description:
The National Audio-Visual Conservation Center (NAVCC) unit of the Library of Congress (LOC) is an audiovisual preservation and digitization facility located on the Packard Campus in Culpeper, VA.

The Video Lab at the NAVCC is tasked with the digitization of multiple tape and disc formats of analog and digital video. It was designed and built to substantially emulate a video production facility and uses an Evertz Master Sync Generator system. This system provides crucial reference video test signals for the video lab. It allows the VTR’s (video tape recorder) to lock on to a reference signal which provides the most optimal conditions for error free digitization of analog video and audio into the digital signal which is then used by the encoders to create archival files. The current system is obsolete and has been damaged beyond repair in certain areas such as the GPS antenna and cabling, which has suffered rodent damage.

The Library needs a contractor that will provide system hardware, installation and documentation for the replacement Master Sync system in the Video Lab at NAVCC.
opps.summary:

The NAVCC is responsible for acquiring, preserving, and providing access to the world’s largest collection of films, television and radio broadcasts, videos, and sound recordings. 

The Video Lab at NAVCC is tasked with digitizing multiple tape and disc formats of analog and digital video using an Evertz Master Sync Generator system, which is currently obsolete and damaged beyond repair.

opps.title: Mexican Criminal Courts Telepresence
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Other Computer Services (Census DWS lane)
opps.description:
Judiciaries in Mexico’s border states (i.e. Baja California Norte, Sonora,Chihuahua, Coahuila, Nuevo Leon, and Tamaulipas) face persistent security and operational challenges when hearing cases involving detainees imprisoned for TCO-related activities. Reliance on in-person hearings requires frequent inmate transport from detention facilities, creating elevated risks of escape, violence, intimidation of judicial personnel, and disruptions to proceedings. These risks contribute to hearing delays, case backlogs, and reduced judicial effectiveness at a time when TCO and FTO-related activity along the U.S.-Mexico border remains acute. The problem stems from limited access to secure, court-approved telepresence technology, uneven digital infrastructure, and procedural frameworks that have not adapted to evolving security threats. Addressing this issue is critical to ensure timely prosecution of high-impact cases.

This project will equip up to four state or federal judiciaries and their courtrooms in Mexico’s northern border states and the corresponding penitentiaries in those states or judicial districts with telepresence systems and secure digital communication platforms.
opps.summary:
Background
 The Mexican Criminal Courts Telepresence project aims to address the operational challenges faced by judiciaries in Mexico's northern border states, specifically Baja California Norte, Sonora, Chihuahua, Coahuila, Nuevo Leon, and Tamaulipas. These challenges arise from the need for in-person hearings involving detainees linked to transnational criminal organizations (TCOs), which pose significant security risks during inmate transport. 

The project seeks to enhance judicial effectiveness by equipping up to four state or federal courtrooms and corresponding penitentiaries with secu
win_reasons: Small business set-aside

opps.title: R499--FY26 Longitudinal Study  Congressionally mandated Longitudinal Study, required per  Public Law 110-389, Section 334
opps.description:
Pre-Solicitation Notice   This notice announces the intent of the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA), Veterans Benefits Administration (VBA), Office of Acquisitions, to issue a solicitation on behalf of the Veteran Readiness and Employment (VR&E) for services in support of Longitudinal Study (PL 110-389 Sec. 334). This project originated with the passage of Public Law 110-389, Section 334.  The longitudinal effort will center on individuals who begin participation in a vocational rehabilitation program under Chapter 31, for fiscal years 2010, 2012, and 2014.  Each cohort will be followed for 20 years.  The scope of this contract is to collect the data on nearly 50,000 Veteran participants and provide analysis that will provide useful demographics and trends relating to the targeted veteran customer within VR&E s Chapter 31 program.  
The Solicitation is set-aside 100% for Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Business (SDVOSB) concerns. Services to be performed under the contemplated contract will be within the North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) Code 541611 with a size standard of $24.5 Million. The Request for Quote (RFQ) will be issued electronically on Contract Opportunities at http://sam.gov.

Only proposals submitted by SDVOSB vendors shall be accepted by the Government. Any proposal that is submitted by an Offeror that is not an SDVOSB shall not be considered for award. VAAR 852.219-73 is included in this solicitation and references the SBA certification database at https://veterans.certify.sba.gov/.Â  However, the website portal at the foregoing link is now static and is no longer being updated as of August 2024.Â  To be eligible for award, a vendor must be listed as SDVOSB certified in the SBA certification database (under Government Certifications) found at the Dynamic Small Business Search website: https://dsbs.sba.gov/search/dsp_dsbs.cfmÂ at the time of proposal submittal and at the time of award, to be considered for award.

opps.title: Managed Attribution Systems (Chameleon)
opps.description:
The Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA) Cyber Support Section (ODV) needs to continue to modernize and streamline DEA’s Internet-based investigative resources with an integrated managed attribution and evidence collection system. Deployment of these capabilities, across the entire DEA will equip agents and analysts with standardized technical tools necessary to safely and effectively conduct online research and collect and preserve evidence.

Conducting Internet-based investigations and research browsing has multiplied over the past several years. With the advent of Drug Trafficking Organizations (DTOs) relying heavily upon Internet-based applications and Dark Web sites to facilitate their criminal enterprise, it is anticipated that in the future, electronic communications will be the preferred and the exclusive means of communication. The threat landscape described creates opportunities for law enforcement to exploit illicit Dark Web market sites utilized by DTOs and dismantle their operations.

Within CISA, the Stakeholder Engagement Division (SED) executes critical responsibilities related to planning, coordinating, and sustaining the Agencyâ€™s engagement with councils, advisory bodies, committees, and other formal governance structures that support national-level critical infrastructure security and resilience.  To execute these functions effectively, SED requires contractor support to plan, manage, operationalize, document, and sustain the full lifecycle of council and advisory activities, as well as to facilitate cross-council coordination, strategic communication, and records and governance management essential to CISAâ€™s broader responsibilities. The forthcoming contract will provide support across:
Â·        Council and Advisory Body Management: Lifecycle management for councils, committees, working groups, and governance artifacts.
Â·        National-Level Coordination Construct Support: Support to evolving cross-sector and interagency governance mechanisms.
Â·        Strategic and Operational Engagement Support: Planning, coordination, logistics, documentation, and execution of classified and unclassified engagements.
Â·        Research, Analysis, and Policy Support: Development of reports, white papers, analyses, dashboards, and executive communications supporting national critical infrastructure priorities.

opps.title: 1DOT Digital Services (supersedes SWES 2.0)
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Custom Computer Programming (web/software — MBDA/Census lane)
opps.description: The U.S. Department of Transportation is establishing a department-wide, multiple-award Blanket Purchase Agreement to modernize its IT infrastructure under the "1DOT" initiative, shifting from siloed legacy systems toward shared, enterprise-level digital platforms. This flexible acquisition vehicle provides specialized software engineering, agile development, cloud-native architecture, and AI/ML solutions to support both mission-specific and department-wide applications. To meet socioeconomic goals while maintaining high-level technical capacity, the requirement utilizes a dual-pool structure that defaults to small business set-asides for most tasks while reserving complex, cross-modal projects for an unrestricted competition pool. Ultimately, this vehicle enables the DOT to rapidly deploy multidisciplinary agile teams to deliver secure, scalable, and human-centered digital services across all its operating administrations. This opportunity supersedes SWES 2.0. To be added to the distribution list for this opportunity, please contact an acquisition member listed on this forecast item. The Draft RFP will be published at GSA eBuy! (https://www.ebuy.gsa.gov/ebuy/).
win_reasons: Agency: BLN24 has past performance here (relationship + credibility advantage)
Partial data/analytics scope match — verify fit vs Census/Education delivery experience
Strong web modernization scope match — BLN24 proven prime (MBDA website $4.4M, Census DWS, Oversight.gov)
Full & Open — competing against all firm sizes; lower win probability without incumbent advantage
OUT OF SCOPE: vehicle — not in BLN24 delivery lanes
⚠️ Gap area detected: AI / Machine Learning, DevSecOps, Cybersecurity — would need a teaming partner for this scope
matched_lanes: Web/Digital Platforms (MBDA $4.4M, Census DWS, Oversight.gov — proven prime)

opps.title: Enterprise Gateway and Integration Services (EGIS) III
opps.description:
USCIS has a requirement for Enterprise Gateway and Integration Services (EGIS) III. Historically, EGIS has served as the enterprise integration backbone connecting USCIS internal systems with Department of Homeland Security (DHS), interagency, and external partners. While this core mission remains critical, EGIS will also expand this role supporting national security, immigration system integrity and advanced adjudication operations with key mission including: National security vetting and evolving immigration policies; screening immigration benefit applicants; protecting immigration benefits and work authorization integrity; advanced data integration, analytics, AI-enabled anomaly detection, and decision support; agility, rapid delivery, operational excellence, transparency, and reduced duplication; fraud detection, risk scoring, and investigative data workflows. 
As of 3/3/2026 we are in the market research/ acquisition planning phase of this acquisition, as such, some of the APFS fields may change over the coming months. This record will continually be updated once changes to the strategy are known.

The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Office of Procurement Operations (OPO) on behalf of the DHS Science and Technology Directorate (S&T) hereby issues this notice of intent to award a sole source contract to Bloomberg Industry Group, Inc, 1801 South Bell Street Arlington, VA 22202.

 Background

In support of the S&T mission, many divisions need access to certain scientific journals and databases to remain at the forefront of their research.  Bloomberg Government is a premier platform for federal policy, budget, and legislative intelligence. The proposed purchase will enable DHS S&T staff to access real-time government data, track legislative developments, and conduct policy analysis, thereby enhancing operational effectiveness and strategic decision-making. The acquisition is expected to streamline research, improve situational awareness, and support mission-critical activities.  S&T will be procure five  (5) subscriptions for (5) users.

Bloomberg Government (BGOV) is a unique, proprietary platform that provides comprehensive, real-time federal legislative, regulatory, and budgetary intelligence specifically tailored for government affairs professionals. No other commercially available product offers the same breadth and depth of integrated data, advanced analytics, real-time alerts, and customizable dashboards in a single solution. BGOV’s exclusive aggregation of congressional activity, appropriations tracking, policy analysis, and federal contract intelligence is essential for DHS S&T’s mission to monitor and respond to rapidly evolving legislative and regulatory developments. opps.agency: NIH Office of Logistics and Acquisition Operations
opps.description:
This is a notice of intent, not a request for a quotation. A solicitation document will not be issued and quotations will not be requested.

This procurement is for a one-year (07/01/2026 – 06/30/2027) contract to provide continued licensing, maintenance, and support for the Careware Nurse Self-Scheduling and Productivity System currently deployed within the NIH Clinical Center Nursing Department. This system improves efficiency in generating monthly staff schedules and provides critical data to support budgetary planning and staffing decisions. This is a sole source requirement to ensure uninterrupted system availability and operational continuity.

This acquisition is being conducted using policies unique to the Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) Part 13 –Simplified Acquisition Procedures, Subpart 13.106-1(b)(1), For purchases not exceeding the simplified acquisition threshold (SAT), contracting officers may solicit from one source if the contracting officer determines that the circumstance of the contract action deem only one source reasonably available. Contracts awarded using FAR Part 13-Simplified Acquisition Procedures are exempt from the requirements of FAR Part 6-Competition Requirements.

Comments to this announcement, referencing this posting number, may be submitted to the NIH, Clinical Center, Office of Purchasing and Contracts, prior to the closing date specified in this announcement, to the attention of Ryssa Nix, Contract Specialist via email to Ryssa.nix@nih.gov by close date.

opps.title: PROGRAM MANAGEMENT AND ANALYTICAL SUPPORT SERVICES FOR THE UNITED STATES COAST GUARD DEPUTY COMMANDANT FOR SYSTEMS (DCS)
opps.description:
The Contractor shall provide all necessary personnel, management, and administrative services to support the Deputy Commandant for Systems (DCS) enterprise. This requirement provides specialized professional services focused on organizational transformation, strategic planning, and comprehensive systems integration support. The primary objective is to secure mission-critical expertise that facilitates effective governance and operational efficiency across the enterprise, ensuring that all DCS initiatives are executed in alignment with strategic objectives and architectural standards.

The scope of work encompasses a diverse array of technical and administrative functions, specifically focusing on Project and Portfolio Management, Strategic Planning and Analysis, and Business Transformation. Key support areas include Data and IT Architecture Transformation, Logistics, System Engineering Lifecycle (SELC) Process Support, and Project Management/Owners Representation Services. Additionally, the Contractor will provide Change Management, Strategic Communications, Governance Support, Analytical Services, Real Estate/Real Property Support, and Facilitation, Coordination, and Documentation Support to ensure seamless mission execution across all DCS lines of effort.

Sources-Sought Notice

The Indian Health Service, Crownpoint Service Unit is issuing this sources-sought notice (SSN) to identify capable Indian-owned, economic enterprises – large (IEE) or small (ISBEE) – that can provide Coding Reference Software Services.  

Other capable firms are also invited to respond to this SSN.  The Contracting Officer will consider responses for Buy Indian Act (25 U.S.C. 47) opportunities first, then for set aside programs under the Small Business Act (41 U.S.C. 3104).

The applicable North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code assigned to this requirement is 541519 – Other Computer Related Services with a small business size standard of 750 Employees.

THIS IS NOT A SOLICITATION. Information collected from this notice is purely for market research only and will be used to determine the contracting office’s acquisition strategy including competition, set asides, and locating Indian-owned economic enterprises.  Submission of any information in response to this market survey is purely voluntary.  The government assumes no financial responsibility for any costs incurred in response to this request for information. 

In accordance with FAR 4.203-1(b), offerors and quoters must be registered in System for Award Management at SAM.gov, at the time an offer or quotation is submitted and at time of award.

opps.title: ISBEE/IEE Sources Sought for Revenue Enhancement Services for the IHS Billings Area
opps.description:
SOURCES SOUGHT NOTICE

This is a Sources Sought notice. This is NOT a solicitation for proposals, proposal abstracts, or quotations. Pursuant to 48 CFR Part 326, Subpart 326.603-3 and IHM 5.5-6, Indian Health Service must use the negotiation authority of the Buy Indian Act, 25 U.S.C. 47, to give preference to Indian Economic Enterprises (IEE) whenever the use of that authority is authorized and practicable.

The Government is seeking to identify Indian Small Business Economic Enterprises (ISBEEs), Indian Economic Enterprises (IEE), and any other category of vendor capable of performing the below Indian Health Services (IHS) requirement. Any vendor responding to this notice should submit their capability statement as well as the Buy Indian Act Indian Economic Enterprise Representation Form (attached).

Consistent with the revised Buy Indian Act, HHS/IHS adopted the definition of an Indian Economic Enterprise (IEE) set forth in 48 CFR 1480.201.  This definition includes the following requirement, amongst others: “the Indians or Indian Tribes must, together, receive at least a majority of the earnings from the contract.”. 

Instructions for submission are outlined further below.

DEFINITIONS

INDIAN ECONOMIC ENTERPRISE (IEE):

Any business activity owned by one or more Indians, Federally Recognized Indian Tribes, or Alaska Native Corporations provided that:


	The combined Indian, Federally Recognized Indian Tribe or Alaska Native Corporation ownership of the enterprise constitutes not less than 51%;
	The Indian, Federal Recognized Indian Tribes, or Alaska Native Corporations must, together, receive at least 51% of the earnings from the contract; and
	The management and daily business operations must be controlled by one or more individuals who are Indians.


DESCRIPTION OF REQUIREMENT

Revenue Enhancement Services

NAICS:

518210 - Computing Infrastructure Providers, Data Processing, Web Hosting, and Related Services

Unique Requirements:

SEE ATTACHMENT:

rex_analysis.what_they_need: Department of Education Institute of Education Sciences (IES) needs a contractor to evaluate the effectiveness of Workforce Pell Grants — a federal program allowing Pell Grant funding for short-term workforce-focused training. Evaluation assesses program design, implementation, and outcomes using rigorous research methodology.
rex_analysis.rex_take: BLN24 is stronger here than the initial score suggests. IRS SB/SE BOD Leadership Initiative ($2.7M via Clarity24) is a DIRECT workforce development / human capital reference — leadership development, culture assessment for 60+ IRS executives, organizational transformation, capacity building. That directly maps to evaluating workforce training programs. EDARMST $2.5M is same agency (IES). Fors Marsh JV covers evaluation methodology via DHRA $59M behavioral research. Teaming play: BLN24/Clarity24 leads workforce dev + data analytics, Fors Marsh JV leads evaluation design. MRAS response is low-effort. Respond now.

opps.title: Forward Networks Unlimited Enterprise License
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Other Computer Services (Census DWS lane)
opps.description:
The Defense Information Systems Agency / Defense Information Technology Contracting Organization, 2300 East Drive, Scott AFB, IL 62225, intends to compete a requirement for proprietary, commercial off-the-shelf, brand-name Forward Networks, Inc., Forward Enterprise software Enterprise Licenses (EL) and associated Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) resident engineering services.

This requirement will be competed as a reseller-provided solution to establish a digital twin of the Department of Defense Information Network (DoDIN) across physical, virtual, and multi-cloud environments for multi-vendor network visualization and prediction. The baseline EL procurement includes two dedicated, embedded OEM resident engineers possessing the proprietary technical expertise necessary to integrate the platform across the complex DoDIN infrastructure. Additionally, the requirement includes an option for further resident engineering support should the agency's operational needs expand beyond the two baseline engineers provided.

The proposed contract action will result in a Firm-Fixed-Price contract. Competition is restricted to authorized resellers of Forward Networks, Inc., and offerors must possess an interim or final Top Secret Facility Clearance from the Defense Counterintelligence and Security Agency (DCSA) at the time of proposal submission. The statutory authority permitting this limitation is Title 10 United States Code 3204(a)(1), as implemented by FAR 6.103-1, Only one responsible source and no other supplies or services will satisfy agency requirements.

The anticipated award date is July 31, 2026. The evaluation methodology selected for this commercial acquisition is Lowest Price Technically Acceptable in accordance with FAR 15.103-2. Further details, including the release date of the solicitation, will be made available exclusively on SAM.gov.

Product or Service Code (PSC) and North American Industry Classification System (NAICS):


	PSC: DA10 - IT and Telec

opps.title: T006--Whole Healthcare Channel Subscriptions Brand Name or Equal  to the C.A.R.E. Channel
opps.description:
This is a combined synopsis/solicitation for commercial products and commercial services as prepared in accordance with the format in Revolutionary Federal Acquisition Regulation Overhaul (RFO) Subpart 12.212(b)(1), as supplemented in the attached Request for Quotation (RFQ). This announcement constitutes the only solicitation. A firm-fixed price purchase order is anticipated.  RFO 12, Acquisition of Commercial Products and Commercial Services will be used. This announcement constitutes the only solicitation. 
The solicitation number is 36C26026Q0766 and is attached.
This solicitation is unrestricted. This solicitation incorporates provisions and clauses by reference. The full text of provisions and clauses may be accessed electronically at www.acquisition.gov. 
The associated North American Industrial Classification System (NAICS) code for this procurement is 512110, with a small business size standard of $40 Million. 
The VA Puget Sound Healthcare System is seeking to purchase Whole Healthcare Channel Subscriptions Brand Name or Equal to the C.A.R.E. Channel for their Tacoma, WA and Seattle, WA locations.
Service Locations: 
See attached solicitation, Section D.1, Statement of Work
The period of performance will be a base plus four option years. 
Base Year:                 08/01/2026   07/31/2027
Option Year One:      08/01/2027   07/31/2028
Option Year Two:     08/01/2028   07/31/2029
Option Year Three:   08/01/2029   07/31/2030
Option Year Four:     08/01/2030   07/31/2031

Award will be based upon the evaluation factors listed in Section E of the attached RFQ. The award will be made to the response most advantageous to the Government. Responses should contain your best terms and conditions. 
Offers must be emailed to Jennifer Robles jennifer.robles1@va.gov and received no later than 1:00 PM Mountain on 07/09/2026. 
Questions to this solicitation shall be submitted via electronic mail to the Contracting Officer Jennifer Robles (jennifer.robles1@va.gov). Question

opps.title: SOLE SOURCE: NGS Atomic Clock Products Evaluation
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Custom Computer Programming (web/software — MBDA/Census lane)
opps.description:
***THE NATIONAL OCEANIC AND ATMOSPHERIC ADMINISTRATION’S (NOAA) ACQUISITION & GRANTS OFFICE, EASTERN ACQUISITION DIVISION-NORFOLK, NATIONAL GEODETIC SURVEY (NGS) OFFICE, INTENDS TO NEGOTIATE AND AWARD A PURCHASE ORDER, ON A SOLE SOURCE BASIS, UNDER AUTHORITY OF RFO FAR 6.103-1, WITH SAINT LOUIS UNIVERSITY FOR ATOMIC CLOCK PRODUCTS EVALUATION TO BE DELIVERED/PERFORMED AT NATIONAL GEODETIC SURVEY, SILVER SPRING, MD. NO SET ASIDE IS TO BE USED FOR THIS SOLE SOURCE REQUIREMENT. THE ASSOCIATED NAICS CODE IS 541511, CUSTOM COMPUTER PROGRAMMING SERVICES.



***THE SOLE SOURCE DETERMINATION IS BASED ON NOAA’S REQUIREMENT THAT SAINT LOUIS UNIVERSITY (SLU) IS THE ONLY VENDOR THAT CAN PROVIDE THIS SERVICE.

SAINT LOUIS UNIVERSITY (SLU), POSSESSES THE UNIQUE TECHNICAL EXPERTISE AND EXISTING SOFTWARE INFRASTRUCTURE REQUIRED TO MEET THE AGENCY'S NEEDS. SLU HAS ALREADY DEVELOPED THE PROTOTYPE PRECISE-CLOCK-ESTIMATION FRAMEWORK SPECIFICALLY DESIGNED TO INTERFACE WITH NGS’S PROPRIETARY MPAGES LIBRARIES. THIS FRAMEWORK WAS THE BASIS FOR PEER-REVIEWED RESEARCH PROVING THAT NGS CAN ACHIEVE CLOCK-PRODUCT PERFORMANCE COMPARABLE TO THE INTERNATIONAL GNSS SERVICE (IGS). THE PROJECT REQUIRES IN-DEPTH EXPERTISE IN GNSS DATA PROCESSING, CLOCK ANALYSIS, AND OPTIMAL ESTIMATION TECHNIQUES THAT ARE HIGHLY SPECIALIZED. SLU IS UNIQUELY POSITIONED DUE TO THEIR INTIMATE FAMILIARITY WITH THE EXISTING MATLAB AND C++ FRAMEWORK THAT MUST NOW BE MODERNIZED AND MIGRATED TO A STANDARD LINUX ENVIRONMENT. SLU INCLUDES STAFF ON THE IGS CLOCK WORKING GROUP. THIS SPECIFIC LEADERSHIP FUNCTION IS CRITICAL FOR THIS PROJECT, WHICH REQUIRES COORDINATING INDEPENDENT VALIDATION AND PERFORMANCE ASSESSMENTS OF NGS CLOCK PRODUCTS WITH THE IGS. SLU HAS ALREADY ESTABLISHED A DEDICATED GNSS STATION AND TESTING ENVIRONMENT SPECIFICALLY FOR THE EVALUATION OF THE SAFRAN SRO-5680 ATOMIC CLOCKS PROVIDED BY NGS. THIS EXISTING SETUP ALLOWS FOR IMMEDIATE COMMENCEMENT OF THE FREQUENCY STABILITY AND ENVIRONME

The task order deliverables will cover all 50 states, three territories, and the District of Columbia, enabling ARNG G-9 to acquire and distribute resources for operations and maintenance of ARNG facilities.

opps.title: AN/TMQ-53 Tactical Meteorological Observing System (TMOS) Contractor Logistics Support (CLS)
opps.description:
The Air Force Life Cycle Management Center (AFLCMC), Aerospace Management Systems Division, Weather Systems Branch (AFLCMC/ESAK), Hanscom AFB, MA 01731, is issuing a formal Request for Proposal (RFP) to provide Tactical Meteorological Observing System (TMOS) Contractor Logistics Support (CLS) for sustainment efforts supporting TMQ-53 systems from FY26 to FY31.

The contractor shall provide worldwide Contractor Logistics Support (CLS) to sustain currently fielded TMQ-53 systems in their baseline configuration. This effort encompasses comprehensive program management, logistics support (including helpdesk, maintenance, and spares/repair management), Operational, Safety, Suitability, and Effectiveness (OSS&E) impact analysis, test and evaluation, Configuration Control Board (CCB) support, and obsolescence and systems engineering. Additionally, the contractor is responsible for tracking and reporting critical system performance metrics (such as System Availability, Mission Capable, and MTBF rates), managing program data (including GFM-A, materials, shipping, and travel), and providing computer resource support, Engineering Change Proposals (ECPs), cybersecurity, technical manual maintenance, and system upgrades due to technical refresh. While worldwide support is required, there is no requirement for contractor travel to overseas locations.

This proposed effort is a total small business set-aside. The Government has determined, IAW FAR Subpart 19.103, that the NAICS classification of 541990 - All Other Professional, Scientific, and Technical Services best describes the principal nature of the product and service being acquired and represents the greatest percentage of the contract price. This classification is assigned a Small Business Association (SBA) size standard of annual receipts of less than $19.5M. If you disagree with this assessment of a total set-aside, please notify the Contracting Officer within seven (7) days of notice.

opps.title: Google Workspace Enterprise Plus
opps.description:
This procurement, supporting the Office of the Chief Information Officer (OCIO), is for the use of Google Workspace Enterprise Plus Apps to support CISAâ€™s IT Operations performance functions. The Enterprise Plus apps are a cloud-based browser with built-in controls that provide encryption and verification which enables work from anywhere and eliminates the need for VPNs. The apps have the ability to operate on a global scale while protecting information from phishing, malware, ransomware, and supply chain attacks. And the apps have secure endpoints that do not require patching and strong account takeover protections.

The software will be used to analyze CISA datasets to enhance leadership awareness of trends and organizational performance. The software procured must be capable of conducting real- time analysis; be relatively easy to use; allow data to flow easily into and out of the software; have forecasting capabilities that can predict values based on trends in the dataset; utilize user- friendly dashboards to format visualizations; and support professional reporting formats and data visualizations suitable for executive-level presentations.

 The contractor shall provide the CISA Office of the Chief Information Officer (OCIO) with the following: 
1. Google Workspace Enterprise Plus; New â€“ Quantity: 1,300, 
2. Google Workspace Enterprise Plus; Archived â€“ Quantity: 1,300, 
3. G Suite Support Base Fee; - Quantity: 1, 
4. G Suite Support Variable Fee; - Quantity: 1,
5. Cloud Platform â€“ Support G Suite; Quantity: 50,000

opps.title: Real Estate Information Database Subscription
opps.description:
Sources Sought
Kansas City District Real Estate Information Database Subscription

The Kansas City District of the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers (NWK) has a requirement to procure actual confirmed and personally verified web-based nationwide real estate sales and lease market data for vacant lands and improved properties of all types (residential, commercial, industrial, retail, office, mixed-use, multifamily and special use properties), in order for the staff appraiser to provide valuation services in support of NWK. Confirmed and personally verified data is information the proprietor of the data has confirmed with a party to the transaction (i.e. brokers, sellers, buyers, etc.) and then verified it to be true and accurate. In order to satisfy customer’s needs, a data provider needs go beyond County records and Multiple Listing Service (MLS) data, by contacting all parties involved with transactions (i.e. buyers, sellers, brokers, appraisers and attorneys) to obtain & verify sales and leasing details.

Per ER 405-1-04, appraisals must conform to: “Guiding Standards. Except as modified in this regulation, the requirements and guidance in the Uniform Appraisal Standards for Federal Land Acquisitions (UASFLA, often called The Yellow Book), and the Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice (USPAP), current edition, as promulgated by the Appraisal Foundation, will be utilized in preparing appraisals for Federal acquisition purposes. USACE appraisals must also comply with the Uniform Relocation Assistance and Real Property Acquisition Policies Act of 1970, Public Law 91-646, as amended. (42 USC 4601 et seq.), (the Uniform Act), codified in 49 CFR Part 24.”

UASFLA states verification rule: “Sales Comparison Approach - The sales comparison approach is normally the preferred method of valuation for property being acquired under these Standards. The sales comparison approach is a systematic procedure in which appraisers study the market

opps.description:
The purpose of this Request for Quote is for the acquisition of replace a SONET Ring at Eight (8) each locations, to include SRS to the appropriate GFE CAT 5e, or Thin Net COAX, or Fiber Optic Patch Panels (FOPP) at each location with the applicable Contractor EFI&T patch cords. All FOPP terminations are Snap-and-Twist (ST) connectors. At all locations, cabinet rack space is available for the Contractor to EFI&T mount their equipment. Each cabinet has spare 110VAC, 60Hz recessed female outlets for the Contractor to EFI&T their SRS's power connections or the required Contractor EFI&T UPS. The Contractor shall EFI&T patch cables from these cabinets to the official GFE Demarcation Point, within the serving Comm Room(s). The Government shall provide cut sheets 10 days after contractor award, detailing circuit terminations for each location and the respective patch panels. The Government
requires a commercial, carrier grade SONET/optical transport solution compatible with existing JB MDL network infrastructure throughout Joint Base McGuire-Dix-Lakehurst (JBMDL), New Jersey, in support of the 87 Communication Squadron (87 CS). This requirement is being solicited in accordance with FAR Part 12, Acquisition of Commercial Products and Commercial Services.

See Attch 1 - Statement of Work, for Specific requirements.

opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Other Computer Services (Census DWS lane)
opps.description:
The U.S. Department of State, Bureau of Diplomatic Technology (DT), Office of Consular Systems and Technology (CST), issues this Request for Information (RFI) for market research and acquisition planning purposes only. This RFI does not constitute a solicitation or commitment to award a contract.

The Department seeks information from qualified vendors capable of delivering a comprehensive Online Presence Vetting (OPV) solution to support consular visa adjudication at a scale of up to 18 million applicants annually across more than 300 consular locations worldwide. The solution must provide automated open-source intelligence (OSINT) data collection, entity resolution, and analytical insights to enable effective online presence screening of visa applicants.

Interested parties must review the attached document RFI_OPV for full requirements and submission guidelines, and must complete and submit Attachment A Response Template along with their response. All responses must be submitted electronically via email to KayaniBA@state.gov no later than 11:00 AM EST on July 14, 2026.

opps.title: Persistent Cyber Training Environment (PCTE) Cyber Range Development Software Application Project (Simulated Internet for Cyber Range Training Events)
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Other Computer Services (Census DWS lane)
opps.description:
Request for Information (RFI)

Title: Persistent Cyber Training Environment (PCTE) Cyber Range Development Software Application Project (Simulated Internet for Cyber Range Training Events [Grey Space])

Issued By: Army Contracting Command Orlando in support of PEO STRI, PM CT2, PdM CRT PCTE

Issue Date: 18 June 2026

Response Due Date: 14 July 2026


	Introduction


The Capability Program Executive Simulation Training, Test and Threat (CPE ST3), Program Manager Cyber Test and Training (PM CT2), Product Manager Cyber Resilience and Training (PdM CRT), Persistent Cyber Training Environment (PCTE) is seeking information from vendors and technology providers regarding a simulated internet capability (often called a Cyber Range or Grey Space Network). The purpose of this RFI is to explore available capabilities with a focus on realism, scale, and control.



This RFI is not a solicitation for proposals but rather a means to gather information for potential future procurement. Vendors are encouraged to provide detailed responses that address the requirements outlined below.



PCTE is built on a hierarchical, two‑plane architecture: a Control Plane (CP) hosts hardened, accredited services (training portal, help‑desk, dashboards, etc.) and provisions resources, while a logically isolated Event Plane (EP) runs virtual machines, containers, and software‑defined networking to create dynamic cyber‑range environments; the platform is deployed across regional compute/storage (RCS) sites and newer enterprise compute/storage (ENT) sites, leveraging VMware Cloud Foundation, NSX‑T for SDN, F5 edge firewalls, and Red Hat SSO for authentication.


	Objectives


The primary objectives of the simulated internet capability are:


	
	
		To create a realistic and highly scalable cyber range environment / Grey Space Network for cybersecurity training and exercises.
		To generate realistic background traffic.
		Capture vendor specify platform's maximum throughput (Gbps) and concurrent se

opps.title: Kronos Software Licenses and Maintenance Support
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Other Computer Services (Census DWS lane)
opps.description: TSA requires the Ultimate Kronos Group (UKG) suite of products to support the electronic Time, Attendance, and Scheduling (eTAS) system. eTAS is an automated system designed to eliminate manual processes and streamline scheduling and timekeeping activities for Transportation Security Officers (TSOs). Deployed across over 400 federalized airports, eTAS is utilized by more than 50,000 TSOs, with timecards processed for payroll submission on a bi-weekly basis (26 times per year). UKG products meet the technical and functional performance requirements necessary to support eTAS operations. These products enable eTAS to communicate with GovTA system hosted by the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Headquarters at the National Finance Center (NFC). ETAS employs custom interfaces to package and transmit timecard information securely from TSA to DHS/NFC. Additionally, UKG products provide TSOs with employee self-service functions, including the ability to view schedules, access timecards, request time off, check accrual balances, and submit shift-trade requests. The software also offers Scheduling Officers a centralized platform to efficiently manage federalized airport operations.

Sources Sought Notice

1.0 INTRODUCTION 
THIS IS NOT A SOLICITATION. The Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) Veterans Benefits Administration (VBA) is issuing this Request for Information (RFI) for Mail Management Services. This RFI is issued solely for information and planning purposes only. There is no obligation for the Government to acquire any products or services described in this RFI. In accordance with the Revolutionary FAR Overhaul (RFO) Subpart 15.101(c), responses to this notice are not offers and cannot be accepted by the Government to form a binding contract. No funds have been authorized, appropriated, or received for this effort. The information provided may be used by VBA in developing its acquisition strategy and informing, refining, and finalizing a Performance Work Statement (PWS). Interested parties are responsible for adequately marking proprietary, restricted or competition sensitive information contained in their response. Only electronic responses to this RFI will be accepted. The Government will not pay for any time expended or information submitted in response to this RFI. Eligibility in participating in a future acquisition does not depend upon a response to this RFI. 
2.0 BACKGROUND 
Through its three (3) administrations, Veterans Benefits Administration (VBA), National Cemetery Administration (NCA), and Veterans Health Administration (VHA), Veterans Affairs (VA) administers vital services to America's Veterans. VA provides health care services, benefits programs, and access to national cemeteries for former military personnel and their dependents.
The fundamental mission of the Veterans Benefits Administration (VBA) is to provide Veterans, service members, and their families the benefits they have earned through their military service to the United States. VBA accomplishes its mission by delivering client-centered, personalized services that help Veterans readjust to civil

The Indian Health Service (IHS) is conducting a SOURCES SOUGHT/REQUEST FOR INFORMATION (RFI) as outlined in Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) Revolutionary FAR Overhaul (RFO) 15.101 to gain knowledge of interest, capabilities, and qualifications of firms especially small businesses including: Native American/Indian-Owned Businesses (Indian Economic Enterprise (IEE)), 8(a) Certified Small Businesses, Historically Underutilized Business Zones (HUB-Zone) Small Businesses, Women Owned Small Businesses, Service Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Businesses (SDVOSB), Small Disadvantaged Businesses (SDB), and Veteran Owned Small Businesses. The RFO can be found here: https://www.acquisition.gov/far-overhaul

THIS NOTICE ALSO SERVES AS THE PRESOLICITATION NOTICE REQUIRED BY FAR RFO Part 5. The Indian Health Service (IHS) intends to issue a Request for Proposals (RFP) for this requirement following completion of market research.

This is a request for a highly configurable commercial-off-the-shelf (COTS) database driven, Web-based Environmental Health Reporting System (Web-EHRS) that will support a nationwide environmental health program. The system will be configured or modified as described in section 2. The system will be utilized at the national, regional, and field levels by approximately 250 environmental health specialists. The contractor must have technical experience or competency in the fields of environmental and public health. The anticipated start of performance date is February 2027.

Attachments:
A10 SS WebEHRS 2027-2032
J01 SOW WEBEHRS 2027-2032
J02 IHS IEE Representation Form

opps.title: Defense Energy Consortium (DECo) - Consortium Management Organization (CMO)
opps.description:
Please only submit a Proposal if you are true Consortium Management Organization (CMO). If you are just intersted in being a member of the consortium once it is established please send an email to the Mr. Brock Moon and Mr. Nicholas Kimmey that you are interested in becoming a member of the eventual consortium and we will pass your information along to the CMO. 

Department of Defense (DoD) installations largely rely on off-site energy providers to support land, air, sea, space, and cyberspace-based national security missions. This dependence creates unacceptable risks, because grid outages caused by maintenance issues, extreme weather, and attacks by determined adversaries may impact our national security missions. To ensure mission continuity even during a grid outage, congress has mandated that DoD achieve by 2030 at least 99.9% energy resilience year-round (i.e., no more than 8.76 hours of downtime annually) for all of its critical missions at all DoD installations (See 10 U.S.C. § 2920).

The Department of the Air Force (DAF) alone has identified nearly 4,000 resilience gaps at its installations worldwide that need rapid and cost-effective mitigation. Meeting this challenge requires DAF to access innovative energy resilience technologies, deploy commercial off-the-shelf technologies, and implement new business models at an enterprise-wide scale with commercial speed.



Concerned that DoD and the military services are unable to access or deploy these technologies and business models at the scale, speed, and cost-effectiveness required to meet its energy resilience challenges, Congress has encouraged DoD to leverage its authority pursuant to 10 U.S.C. § 4022, to partner with a third-party consortium dedicated to military installation resilience and energy innovation.[1]

10 U.S.C. § 4022 authorizes DoD to conduct prototype projects that are directly relevant to enhancing DoD’s mission effectiveness. Other Transaction Authority (OTA) is an al

opps.title: Global Distribution System (Reservation System)
opps.description:
The TSA business process of providing a global distribution system (GDS) to schedule, make hotel reservation and secure flight itineraries/tickets through a direct connectivity reservation system, is a mission critical process.   It is imperative this reservation system be US-based because of the strict security requirements.   TSA has direct accountability to provide efficient and effective bookings on commercial carriers, with the ability to select seating in order to address in a fluid and timely (up to the minute) manner, all threats, new and old.  The computer software reservation system must provide a full range of travel services necessary to support the government at multiple facilities.

The anticipate period of performance is for a base one-year period, with four (4) one-year periods.
