opps.title: OER - NOAA Ship Telepresence (FY27 - FY30)
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Custom Computer Programming (web/software — MBDA/Census lane)
opps.description: This license will provide video/imagery telepresence support for OER Ocean Expeditions on NOAA Ship Okeanos Explorer, and replacement NOAA Ship Discoverer and other platforms/projects; satellite upload / download ship to shore of large data volumes and video streams, with critical live communication to/from shore subject matter experts, universities, cooperative institutes, and outside agencies to expand exploration and understanding of the ocean environment, and allow for immediate awareness and distribution of findings to the ocean community at-large. While video is archived at NOAA NCEI, there is no comparable viewable portal to access the video for the ocean community at large or to reach global subject matter experts to identify findings. This mechanism serves as the publicly viewable access point for video and images that are acquired on the NOAA Ship and through other OER funded projects. Annotated video data from other platforms

IRS SB/SE BOD Leadership Initiative ($2.7M via Clarity24) is a DIRECT workforce development / human capital reference — leadership development, culture assessment for 60+ IRS executives, organizational transformation, capacity building. That directly maps to evaluating workforce training programs. EDARMST $2.5M is same agency (IES). Fors Marsh JV covers evaluation methodology via DHRA $59M behavioral research. opps.title: Electrical Law Library (ELL)
opps.description:
This is a pre-solicitation notice of a proposed contract action issued in accordance with part 12 – Acquisition of Commercial Products and Commercial Services. This is not a solicitation or invitation for offerors to submit bids.

The Federal Bureau of Prisons (FBOP), located at 320 First St. NW, Washington DC, anticipates issuance of request for quotation (RFQ) 15BPCC26Q00000020 for a single award firm-fixed price (FFP) definitive contract for electronic legal research to be utilized all Bureau of Prisons institutions throughout the United States.

The performance period will include a one-year base followed by one option period with an Option to Extend Services FAR 52.217-8.

This acquisition is not set-aside for small business concerns. This will be solicited open market on an unrestricted basis. The NAICS code for this requirement is 519290 – Web Search Portals and All Other Information Services and PSC Code R605 – Library Services.

A solicitation will be issued on or about March 19, 2026, and will be posted to SAM.gov, with an estimated closing date of March 26, 2026. Hard copies of the solicitation will not be made available.  The website provides download instructions.  Any secure/locked documents (if applicable) uploaded with the solicitation will require an active Sam.gov vendor registration for access.  If you encounter problems accessing these documents, please contact the Federal Service Desk at 866-606-8220.  All future information about this solicitation, including any amendments, will also be distributed solely through this website.  Interested parties are responsible for continuously monitoring this website to ensure that they have the most recent information about this proposed contract action.

IMPORTANT: Any contractor interested in obtaining a contract award with the Federal Bureau of Prisons must be registered in the System for Award Management (SAM) at www.sam.gov. All offerors are urged to double check their SAM registration

opps.title: Presolicitation CAPE EVAMOSC Platform Support -
opps.description:
Washington Headquarters Services (WHS) Acquisition Directorate (AD) intends to procure Enterprise Visibility and Management of Operating and Support Cost (EVAMOSC) Platform Support Services. The Government anticipates awarding a single award Indefinite Delivery Indefinite Quantity (IDIQ) with a 5-year ordering period. The EVAMOSC Support Services requirement will be solicited on a “Full and Open” basis. The anticipated effective date of the award is August 8, 2026.



The Government requires EVAMOSC Support Services  to support the Office of the Secretary of War Cost Assessment and Program Evaluation (OSW-CAPE) mission to provide the Department of War (DoW) with timely, insightful and unbiased analysis on resource allocation and cost estimation problems to deliver the optimum portfolio of military capabilities through efficient and effective use of each taxpayer dollar. In achieving this mission, one of OSW-CAPE’s primary goal is to provide data driven insight into the costs of weapon system programs. This detailed analysis enables responsible budgeting and proactive management decisions so that the Department can control cost and achieve savings. In recent years, the DoW and Congress has focused on understanding the costs incurred by weapons systems during the sustainment phase and underlying causes of these cost outcomes, culminating in Congress passing FY26 NDAA, Section 1803  (Major weapon systems: assessment, management, and control of operating and support costs) which assigns responsibility for “developing and maintaining a database on… actual operating and support costs for major weapon systems” to the Director, CAPE. To meet this congressional requirement, OSW-CAPE  obtain support to design, implement, operate, and perform platform sustainment services on the Enterprise Visibility and Management of Operating and Support Costs (EVAMOSC) data system. ). The anticipated requirement will sustain the current EVAMOSC platform

opps.title: R699 - Sole Source Administrative Support Services with Precision Collective and 8a vendor.
opps.description:
This is a COMBINED SYNOPSIS/SOLICITATION for commercial items prepared in accordance with the information in FAR part 12 Acquisition of Commercial Products and Commercial Services. This announcement constitutes the only solicitation. This combined synopsis/solicitation SHALL be posted on both the System for Award Management (SAM) website (https://sam.gov/) and the Navy Electronic Commerce Online (NECO) website (https://www.neco.navy.mil/). This solicitation documents and incorporates provisions and clauses in effect through FAC 2026-01 and DFARS Publication Notice 20260507. It is the responsibility of the contractor to be familiar with the applicable clauses and provisions. The clauses may be accessed in full text at: https://www.acquisition.gov.
This solicitation is a request for quotations (RFQ) and is for commercial services. The RFQ number is N0018926QD094 and is an attachment to this posting. 

The NAICS code is 541611 and the Small Business Standard is $24.5M. 

The Naval Supply System Command (NAVSUP) Fleet Logistics Center Norfolk (FLCN), Pentagon Directorate Contracting Department hereby provides notice of its intent to award a sole source, firm-fixed price (FFP) service contract to Precision Collective (8CBM2) an 8a vendor. Since the estimated cost of this requirement is not expected to exceed $5.5 M, in accordance with FAR 19.805-1(a)(2), these Administrative Assistant services will be procured on a single source 8a basis with Precision collective.
Quoters must be registered in the SAM database to be considered for award. Registration is free and can be completed on-line at http://www.sam.gov/. 
Interested vendors may submit a response to this combined synopsis/solicitation posting and will be considered. However, a determination to not compete this requirement will be at the Government’s discretion. Reimbursement or payment for submission will not be provided. 

The announcement will close at 4:00 PM Eastern Daylight Time (EDT) on 23 July 2026.

opps.title: Pre-Solicitation Notice GPO Production Environment Track and Trace Platform
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Custom Computer Programming (web/software — MBDA/Census lane)
opps.description:
Notice Type: Pre-Solicitation Notice

This is a pre-solicitation notice issued per Materials Management Acquisition Regulation (MMAR) 5.204. The purpose of this notice is to inform industry of the Government’s planned requirement and upcoming solicitation.

This notice is not a Request for Proposals, Request for Quotations, or Invitation for Bids. The Government is not requesting proposals in response to this notice. 

This notice does not constitute a solicitation and does not obligate the Government to issue a solicitation, award a contract, or reimburse any costs incurred in preparing a response. The Government reserves the right to modify or cancel this requirement at any time.

Any formal solicitation, if issued, will be posted separately through SAM.gov. Interested vendors are encouraged and responsible for monitoring SAM.gov for future notices or solicitations related to this notice.

The Government intends to issue a solicitation for a commercially available or configurable track and trace software solution to support end-to-end visibility, monitoring, reporting, and accountability of materials, assets, products, or shipments throughout the supply chain.
opps.summary:
Background
 The U.S. Government Publishing Office (GPO) is planning to issue a solicitation for a commercially available or configurable track and trace software solution. This initiative aims to enhance end-to-end visibility, monitoring, reporting, and accountability of materials, assets, products, or shipments throughout the supply chain.

The notice serves to inform industry stakeholders of the upcoming requirement and does not constitute a solicitation or request for proposals.

opps.title: National-Level Vehicle Data for NHTSA's Database & Analysis
opps.description: The objective of this contract is to provide comprehensive vehicle registration data and supplementary information, including Vehicle Identification Numbers (VINs) for vehicles lacking VINs in certain crash data records, registration data aggregated by zip code, transaction prices for registered vehicles, and other relevant data. This enhanced dataset will support and augment the Agency's crash data collection and analysis capabilities, enabling more informed decision-making and improved data-driven insights.
